## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(Έ¦, E_Έ¦)`** βÄî finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal βÄî not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, β³Λ^d, βÄΠ) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallβÄ™LiebβÄ™Mattis theorem, Miyao 2021
¬ß3, βÄΠ) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `Έ¦ : Type*` together with `[Fintype Έ¦]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `β³Λ` or `β³Λ^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : Έ¦ βÜ£ Έ¦ βÜ£ β³²` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

Eigenvector uniqueness (#854) at fixed `ΈΦ` and eigenvalue uniqueness (#856, via dressed-sector symmetry + Rayleigh identity). Bundled real-form ground-state theorems: same-`ΈΦ` form (#855) and forced-eigenvalue form (#859). Complex-form bridge: complex sector matrix Hermiticity + real-βÜî-complex eigenvector correspondence (#857, #858, #861). Complex-form existence (#860), Marshall-positive uniqueness (#862), and **strongest bundled COMPLEX ground-state theorem** `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full` (#865) βÄî the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the magnetization sector. Generic spin `S`, arbitrary bipartite-antiferromagnetic Heisenberg coupling supported on a connected bipartite graph, with the intermediate-existence hypothesis. The next step is the lift from the magnetization sector to the FULL Hilbert space βÄî comparing ground-state energies across magnetization sectors. | Done |
| P1n (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, final statement) | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 (LiebβÄ™Mattis, general spin-S, `\|A\| βâ† \|¬§A\|`) βÄî final statement.** The current `Prop` definition `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` is the structural, `h_intermediate`-free statement: it closes the conclusion from the physical hypotheses `(1 βâΛ N, 1 βâΛ \|A\|, 1 βâΛ \|¬§A\|)` rather than the older vacuous-at-`N=1` intermediate-support hypothesis. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 | Truly-unconditional closure at all `N βâΞ 1` (PR #3891 toy, PR #3893 general) |
| P1o (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, MattisβÄ™Nishimori anisotropic) | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 (MattisβÄ™Nishimori)**: ground-state uniqueness + `≈€¬≥_tot \|ΈΠ_GSβü© = 0` for the anisotropic XXZ + single-ion Hamiltonian (2.5.14) on a connected bipartite lattice with `\|A\| = \|B\|`, under (i) `βà£1 < ΈΜ βâΛ 1, D βâΞ 0` or (ii) `ΈΜ βâΞ 1, D βâΛ 0`. The model is only U(1)-invariant (not SU(2)), so the proof uses even/odd-sector PerronβÄ™Frobenius (at most double degeneracy) + a deformation argument from the SU(2) point `(ΈΜ,D)=(1,0)` rather than MLM directly. (#3824βÄ™#3837): (e) parity-block matrix irreducibility (#3824), (f) bridge layer giving complex dressed parity-block submatrix `finrank β³² βâΛ 1` (#3825βÄ™#3831) + block-diag bridge (#3832), (g) ΈΦ) = βàë_p finrank β³² (eig M.submatrix_p ΈΦ)` for parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `P`, (h.4) #3844 submatrix-based βâΛ 2 wrappers. **(i) chain: Hermitian spectral chain** (#3846βÄ™#3855): (i.1) #3846 submatrix Hermiticity, (i.2) #3847 submatrix eigenvalue realness (`ΈΦ.im = 0`), (i.3) #3848 submatrix minimum eigenvalue exists, (i.4) #3849 Hermitian eigenspace `= βäΞ` below min, (i.5) #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= βäΞ` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `βâΛ 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `βâΛ 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `ΡΛ'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 bare anisotropic `ΡΛ` specialisation via axis-swap (general `N` with `AxisSwapUnitaryS` + spin-1/2 instance) βÄî the bridge to unconditional `βâΛ 2` modulo PF eigenvalue identification with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`. **(j) chain: PF eigenvalue identification** (#3857βÄ™#3861): (j.1) #3857 PF positive eigenvector exists for unshifted dressed submatrix, (j.2) #3858 lift to complex, (j.3) #3859 `hermitianMinEigenvalue βâΛ ΈΦ` from eigenvector existence, (j.4) #3860 packaged form, (j.5) #3861 consumer-friendly conditional bound at `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (needs the `ΈΫ_PF = hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzβÄ™Wielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank β³² βâΛ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D βâΞ 0` version because single-ion `¬±2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D βâΞ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **ΈΜ=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|Έ¦|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `ΈΜ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `ΈΜ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 βâΛ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `ΈΜ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `ΈΜ βâΞ 1`, `D βâΛ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M βà© Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M βà© Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t βàà Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ¬±2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 βâΛ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ΈΜ < 1`, `D βâΞ 0`; `ΈΜ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ΈΜ`, `D βâΛ 0`. |

(11.4.30)), `siteTranslationPerm_zero`; step 1 toward the spin-wave bounds (axiom-free); **fermionic translation operator (`FermionicTranslation.lean`, eq. (11.4.30), step 2)**: `translationJwSign œÄ œÉ = (-1)^(occupied inversions of œÄ)` (JW fermion sign, `¬±1`) + `translationOperator K z` (signed permutation operator) + `translationOperator_mulVec_basisVec` (the defining action `œ³Χ²_z|œÉβü©=ΈΒ(œÄ,œÉ)|œÉβà‰œÄβ¹Μ¬Ιβü©`) + `translationOperator_zero` (`z=0β΅£1`); axiom-free, `E_SW(k)` + axiom 11.19 to follow); **¬ß11.4.2 Theorem 11.19 (spin-wave bounds, AXIOMATIZED, `SpinWaveExcitation.lean`)**: `momentumPhase K k = e^{-2œÄik/(K+1)}`, `spinWaveEnergy K H k` = `E_SW(k)` (min energy in `≈€^z=S_maxβà£1` βà© `œ³Χ²`-momentum-`k` eigenspace) + `axiom nonsingular_theorem_11_19` βÄî `βàÉ ΈΫβ²¹,ΈΖβ²¹,ΈΨβ²¹,ΈΨβ²²,aβ²¹..bβ²É>0` s.t. under (11.4.31)/(11.4.32) the dispersion `E_SW(k)βà£E_min(S_max)` is two-sided bounded by `F¬Ζ2ΈΫβ¹¥U(1βà£cos k)` (eq. (11.4.33); `Fβ²¹/Fβ²²` (11.4.34)/(11.4.35)); deferred. Completes ¬ß11.4.2); **¬ß11.4.3 Theorem 11.20 (non-singular Hubbard ferromagnetism, AXIOMATIZED, `TasakiNonsingularFerro.lean`)** βÄî the ¬ß11.4 culmination: `tasakiNonsingularHamiltonian = flatBandHamiltonian βà£ sβÄΔΈΘΟΔβÄ†ΟΔ` (eq. (11.4.38); `s=0 β΅£ flat-band`) + `axiom tasaki_theorem_11_20` (`d=1`: `βàÄ ΈΫ>0, βàÉ` thresholds uniform in `K` s.t. `t/s,U/s` large β΅£ `exhibitsFerromagnetism` at `S_max=N/2`); proof via the frustration-free `ΡΞ_p`/Lemmas 11.21-22 + reduction to Theorem 11.11, deferred; **the proof-internal Lemmas 11.21/11.22/11.23 are now axiomatized** in `NonsingularLocalHamiltonian.lean`: `nonsingularLocalHamiltonian` (`ΡΞ_p`, eq. (11.4.48)) + `nonsingular_lemma_11_21` (`βàÄp ΡΞ_pβâΞ0 β΅£ exhibitsFerromagnetism`), `nonsingular_lemma_11_22` (conditions β΅£ `βàÄp ΡΞ_pβâΞ0`), `nonsingular_lemma_11_23` (`t,UβÜëβàû` limit positivity). **¬ß11.4 fully axiomatized incl. all numbered lemmas**); **per-site fermionic spin operators (`FermionSiteSpin.lean`, towards ¬ß11.5)**: `fermionSiteSpinPlus/Minus/Z N i` + `fermionSpinDot N i j` (`≈€_x¬Ζ≈€_y`) βÄî building blocks for the t-J Heisenberg term (eq. (11.5.4)); axiom-free); **¬ß11.5.2 the ferromagnetic t-J model + Proposition 11.24 (AXIOMATIZED, `TJModel.lean`, Issue #4198)**: `fermionSiteNumber N i` (`nΧ²_x`), **`tJHamiltonian N G œ³ J`** = `βà£œ³ PΧ²hc (ΈΘ_{βü®x,yβü©,œÉ}ΡâβÄ†Ρâ) PΧ²hc + J ΈΘ_{βü®x,yβü©}(nΧ²_x nΧ²_y/4 βà£ ≈€_x¬Ζ≈€_y)` (eq. (11.5.4); hopping = `hubbardKineticOnGraph` sandwiched by `hubbardHardcoreProjection`, exchange via `fermionSpinDot`; `œ³,J>0`); **`axiom proposition_11_24`** (d=1 periodic `cycleGraph (N+1)`, `Ne<L` odd β΅£ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N (groundSubmoduleAtFilling βÄΠ) Ne` βÄî i.e. ground states `S_tot=Ne/2` **and** `Ne+1`-fold degeneracy, both via the shared predicate reused from Mielke 11.13 / general flat-band 11.15) βÄî PerronβÄ™Frobenius / spin-charge-separation proof deferred; model axiom-free); **generic fixed-filling hard-core ground subspace (`GroundSubspaceAtFilling.lean`)**: `fillingHardcoreStates`/`groundEnergyAtFilling`/`groundSubmoduleAtFilling` (`H`-eigenspace at the ground energy βä™ `NΧ²=Ne` βä™ `hubbardHardcoreSubspace`), shared by Prop 11.24 and Thm 11.26; axiom-free; **¬ß11.5.3 d=1 decorated Hubbard model + Lemma 11.25 + Theorem 11.26 (AXIOMATIZED, `MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4198)**: the duplicated-internal-site lattice `Έ¦=EβàΣ(IΟ½{1,2})` in `Fin(3K+3)` (`decExternalSite`/`decInternalSite1/2`); localized states `decAlpha`/`decBeta1`/`decBeta2` (eqs. (11.5.7)βÄ™(11.5.9)) + fermion ops `decA/BCreation/Annihilation` (eq. (11.5.10)); `decHopping` (`t ΈΘ bΧ²βÄ†bΧ² βà£ s ΈΘ_{βü®p,qβü©βààE,œÉ}ΟΔβÄ†_pΟΔ_q`, eq. (11.5.14)) + `decInteraction` (`U ΈΘ nΧ²βÜënΧ²βÜ™`, eq. (11.5.13)) + `decHubbardHamiltonian`; **`axiom lemma_11_25`** (`t,UβÜëβàû` Hubbard βâΓ `JβÜëβàû` t-J at `œ³=(1+4ΈΫ¬≤)s`, rendered as spin-structure transfer: Hubbard ground subspace is the maximal-spin `Ne+1`-multiplet **iff** the t-J one is, in the limits) + **`axiom theorem_11_26`** (d=1, `NeβâΛK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NβâΛL`) β΅£ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian βÄΠ) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` βÄî ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); model axiom-free, the two limit theorems documented axioms); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`Ρà_œÉ(œÜ)=ΈΘ_x œÜ(x)Ρâ_{x,œÉ}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **¬ß11.5.4 TanakaβÄ™Tasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) βÄî ¬ß11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `Έ¦=EΟ½{1,2,3}βàΣIΟ½{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)βÄ™(11.5.22), `ΟΔ` with `1/βàö(3+4ΈΫ¬≤)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`ΈΘ_{βü®p,qβü©}(βà£s ΟΔβÄ†ΟΔβà£t bΧ²βÄ†bΧ²)+uβ²¹ΈΘbΧ²βÄ†bΧ²+uβ²²ΈΘdΧ²βÄ†dΧ²`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uβ²²,UβÜëβàû` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`dΧ²ΈΠ=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uβ²²,U=βàû` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uβ²¹>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1βâΛNeβâΛ2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dβâΛNβâΛ2L^d`) β΅£ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian βÄΠ) Ne βä™ ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` βÄî taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uβ²²,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

**Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `Ο²` is `βâΞ0` iff all eigenvalues `βâΞ0`), **A.5** (`Ο²,BΧ²βâΞ0 β΅£ Ο²+BΧ²βâΞ0`), **A.6** (`BΧ²βÄ†BΧ²βâΞ0`, and conversely every `Ο²βâΞ0` is `Ρà¬≤` for a *unique* PSD square root `Ρà=βàöΟ²` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/TasakiAppendixA/PosSemidefBasics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `Ο²βâΛBΧ²` β΅£ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `Ο².eigenvaluesβ²Ä i βâΛ BΧ².eigenvaluesβ²Ä i`; documented axiom, `Math/TasakiAppendixA/EigenvalueMonotone.lean` βÄî mathlib has only the extreme-eigenvalue variational principle). **Corollary A.8** (`Ο²βâΛBΧ² β΅£ Tr e^Ο² βâΛ Tr e^BΧ²`, eq A.2.31; documented axiom, `TraceExpMonotone.lean`, resting on A.7). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `ΡΛ=ΈΘΡΞβ±Φ`, `ΡΞβ±ΦβâΞΈΒβ±Φ`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate β΅£ ground state at `ΈΘΈΒβ±Φ`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`Ο²βâΞ0 βàß βü®ΈΠ|Ο²|ΈΠβü©=0 β΅£ Ο²ΈΠ=0`; `Ο²=BΧ²βÄ†BΧ² β΅£ BΧ²ΈΠ=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `Ρ¥¬≤ΈΠ=0 β΅£ Ρ¥β¹ΫαΒÖβ¹ΨΈΠ=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidefKernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`ΡΛ_v=ΡΛβ²Ä+vVΧ²`, `VΧ²βâΞ0`: `vβÜëβàû` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `ΈΠ` satisfy the effective SchrΟΕdinger eq `PΧ²β²ÄΡΛβ²ÄΈΠ=EΈΠ` on `Hβ²Ä=ker VΧ²` βÄî documented axiom, `EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto, non-vacuous finite-energyβ΅£effective direction). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **¬ßA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) βÄî operator identity `Ρ¥β¹ΜΡ¥β¹Κ=Ρ¥¬≤βà£Ρ¥¬≥(Ρ¥¬≥+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`βà£JβâΛM<J`, `ΈΠβâ†0` β΅£ `Ρ¥β¹ΚΈΠβâ†0`) + raising **membership** (`Ρ¥β¹ΚΈΠβààH_{J,M+1}`, via `[Ρ¥¬≥,Ρ¥β¹Κ]=Ρ¥β¹Κ` + `[Ρ¥¬≤,Ρ¥β¹Κ]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`Ρ¥β¹ΚΡ¥β¹Μ=Ρ¥¬≤βà£Ρ¥¬≥(Ρ¥¬≥βà£1)` eq A.3.8, `Ρ¥β¹ΜΈΠβâ†0` for `βà£J<MβâΛJ`, `Ρ¥β¹ΜΈΠβààH_{J,Mβà£1}`) **proved** βÄî A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularLadder.lean`). Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order. |

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
¬ß2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(œÉ^Έ±)¬≤ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(œÉ^Έ±)βÄ† = œÉ^Έ±` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(œÉ^Έ±)¬≤ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `œÉ^x œÉ^y = i¬ΖœÉ^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `œÉ^Έ± œÉ^Έ≤ + œÉ^Έ≤ œÉ^Έ± = 0` (Έ± βâ† Έ≤) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### Zβ²² Ο½ Zβ²² representation (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The Zβ²² Ο½ Zβ²² structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### 3D rotation matrices `R^(Έ±)_œÄ` (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 eq. (2.1.28))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.28), p. 18 and Problem 2.1.f.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi` | 3Ο½3 real orthogonal œÄ-rotation matrices | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi_sq` | `(R^(Έ±)_œÄ)¬≤ = 1` | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi_mul_rot3D{2,3,1}Pi` | `R^(Έ±)_œÄ ¬Ζ R^(Έ≤)_œÄ = R^(Έ≥)_œÄ` (cyclic, Problem 2.1.f) | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi_comm_*` | distinct-axis `R^(Έ±)_œÄ` and `R^(Έ≤)_œÄ` commute | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |

| `pauli_linearIndep` | `{1, œÉ^x, œÉ^y, œÉ^z}` is linearly independent | `Quantum/SpinHalfDecomp.lean` |

### Polynomial-basis decomposition for S = 1 (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum
Many-Body Systems*, ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15 + solution S.1, p. 493.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}` | the three diagonal projectors `\|œà^œÉβü©βü®œà^œÉ\|` (œÉ βàà {+1, 0, -1}) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}_eq_polynomial` | each diagonal projector equals a polynomial in `≈€^(3)` (Lagrange interpolation) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|œà^œ³βü©βü®œà^œÉ\|` (œ³ βâ† œÉ) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/βàö2) ≈€^- ¬Ζ P_œÉ` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/βàö2) ≈€^+ ¬Ζ P_œÉ` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (≈€^-)¬≤ ¬Ζ P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (≈€^+)¬≤ ¬Ζ P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3Ο½3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S βâΞ 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : β³ï`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) β³²`, with `N = 2S : β³ï` (so `N = 1` β΅î `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` β΅î `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `≈€^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, βÄΠ, -N/2)` for `N : β³ï` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `βàö(k¬Ζ(N βà£ k + 1))` (resp. `βàö((N βà£ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `≈€^(1) := (≈€^+ + ≈€^-) / 2`, `≈€^(2) := (≈€^+ βà£ ≈€^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `≈€^+` annihilates `\|Nβü©` (highest weight); `≈€^-` annihilates `\|0βü©` (lowest weight) βÄî the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[≈€^{(3)}, ≈€^+] = ≈€^+` and `[≈€^{(3)}, ≈€^-] = -≈€^-`: `≈€^¬±` shift the magnetic quantum number by `¬±1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (Έ≤-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(≈€^+)α¥¥ = ≈€^-`, `(≈€^-)α¥¥ = ≈€^+`, and Hermiticity of `≈€^{(Έ±)}` for `Έ± βàà {1, 2, 3}` (Έ≤-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `≈€^{(1)}` and `≈€^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(Έ¥_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(≈€^{(3)} βà£ ΈΜ_j βÄΔ 1) ¬Ζ P_k = (ΈΜ_k βà£ ΈΜ_j) ¬Ζ P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (Έ≤-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `βàè_{j βâ† k} (≈€^{(3)} βà£ ΈΜ_j βÄΔ 1) / (ΈΜ_k βà£ ΈΜ_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `≈€^{(3)} ¬Ζ P_k = ΈΜ_k ¬Ζ P_k` (where `ΈΜ_k = (N : β³²)/2 βà£ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ¬Ζ ≈€^{(3)} = ΈΜ_k ¬Ζ P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (Έ≤-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ¬Ζ P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ¬Ζ A`). Specialised to `≈€^¬±` produces off-diagonal matrix units (Έ≤-6 of Issue #458) βÄî the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `≈€^+ ¬Ζ P_{k+1} ¬Ζ ≈€^- = (k + 1)(N βà£ k) ¬Ζ P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `≈€^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1Χ², ≈€^{(Έ±)}}` (Έ≤-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `≈€^+ ¬Ζ P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `≈€^- ¬Ζ P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `≈€^¬±` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (Έ≤-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `ΈΘ_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (Έ≤-6, Έ≤-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(β³²)` (Έ≤-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `≈€^+ ¬Ζ P_{i+1} = βàö((i+1)(Nβà£i)) ¬Ζ E_{i, i+1}` and `≈€^- ¬Ζ P_i = βàö((Nβà£i)(i+1)) ¬Ζ E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (Έ≤-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `≈€^¬±` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(≈€^{(3)})¬≤ = diag((N/2 βà£ i)¬≤)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(≈€^{(1)})¬≤ + (≈€^{(2)})¬≤ + (≈€^{(3)})¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬Ζ 1` for general spin (Έ≤-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `≈€^+ ¬Ζ ≈€^- = diag((i + 1)(N βà£ i))`. The product is diagonal because `≈€^+[i, l] ¬Ζ ≈€^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `≈€^- ¬Ζ ≈€^+` and `(≈€^{(3)})¬≤`, this assembles the Casimir identity (Έ≤-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `≈€^- ¬Ζ ≈€^+ = diag(i ¬Ζ (N βà£ i + 1))`. Symmetric to Έ≤-12 with `≈€^¬±` swapped (Έ≤-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(≈€^{(1)})¬≤ + (≈€^{(2)})¬≤ + (≈€^{(3)})¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬Ζ 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ¬Ζ 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(≈€^{(1)})¬≤ + (≈€^{(2)})¬≤ = (1/2)(≈€^+ ¬Ζ ≈€^- + ≈€^- ¬Ζ ≈€^+)` is proved using `module` (Έ≤-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `≈€^{(3)} ¬Ζ \|kβü© = (N/2 βà£ k) ¬Ζ \|kβü©`, where `\|kβü© := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) βÜ£ β³²)` (Έ≤-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `≈€^+ ¬Ζ \|kβü© = βàö(k(N βà£ k + 1)) ¬Ζ \|k βà£ 1βü©` for `k βâΞ 1`, and `≈€^- ¬Ζ \|kβü© = βàö((N βà£ k)(k + 1)) ¬Ζ \|k + 1βü©` for `k βâΛ N βà£ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (Έ≤-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(≈€)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|kβü© = (N(N+2)/4) ¬Ζ \|kβü©`. Direct consequence of `(≈€)¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬Ζ 1` (Έ≤-14) βÄî every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (Έ≤-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `≈€^{(Έ±)}` and `≈€^¬±` commutes with the Casimir `(≈€)¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬Ζ 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (Έ≤-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[≈€^+, ≈€^-] = 2 ¬Ζ ≈€^{(3)}`. Combines Έ≤-12 (`≈€^+ ¬Ζ ≈€^- = diag((i + 1)(N βà£ i))`) and Έ≤-13 (`≈€^- ¬Ζ ≈€^+ = diag(i (N βà£ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) βà£ i(N-i+1) = N βà£ 2i = 2(N/2 βà£ i)` matches `2 ¬Ζ ≈€^{(3)}` (Έ≤-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[≈€^{(1)}, ≈€^{(2)}] = i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `≈€^{(1)} = (≈€^+ + ≈€^-)/2` and `≈€^{(2)} = (≈€^+ βà£ ≈€^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) βà£ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ≈€^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (Έ≤-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[≈€^{(2)}, ≈€^{(3)}] = i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan Έ≤-2 (`[≈€^{(3)}, ≈€^¬±] = ¬±≈€^¬±`) via `≈€^{(2)} = (≈€^+ βà£ ≈€^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-≈€^+ βà£ ≈€^-) = (-1/(2i)) ¬Ζ 2 ≈€^{(1)} = i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(1)}` (Έ≤-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[≈€^{(3)}, ≈€^{(1)}] = i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan Έ≤-2 via `≈€^{(1)} = (≈€^+ + ≈€^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (≈€^+ βà£ ≈€^-) = (1/2) ¬Ζ (2i) ¬Ζ ≈€^{(2)} = i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(2)}`. Together with Έ≤-20 and Έ≤-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (Έ≤-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kβü©βü®k|`: `P_k ¬Ζ P_k = P_k` and `P_i ¬Ζ P_j = 0` for `i βâ† j`. Combined with Έ≤-9 (`βàë_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `≈€^{(3)}` (Έ≤-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (Έ≤-25+) βÄî lets us pull a polynomial in `≈€^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (Έ≤-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (≈€^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = βàè_{j βâ† k} (≈€^{(3)} βà£ ΈΜ_j βÄΔ 1)/(ΈΜ_k βà£ ΈΜ_j)` with `ΈΜ_j = (N : β³²)/2 βà£ j.val`. Combines Έ≤-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `≈€^{(3)}`**, which combined with Έ≤-9 (`βàë P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1Χ²` (Έ≤-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `≈€^+ = ≈€^{(1)} + i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(2)}` and `≈€^- = ≈€^{(1)} βà£ i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(2)}`. The defining identities `≈€^{(1)} = (1/2)(≈€^+ + ≈€^-)`, `≈€^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(≈€^+ βà£ ≈€^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (Έ≤-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k βàà Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k βàà Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(1)}, ≈€^{(2)}, ≈€^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (Έ≤-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (Έ≤-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(Έ±)}}`**: `≈€^+, ≈€^- βàà Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(1)}, ≈€^{(2)}, ≈€^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of Έ≤-26 (`≈€^¬± = ≈€^{(1)} ¬± i ¬Ζ ≈€^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `β³²`-linear combinations (Έ≤-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(Έ±)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} βàà Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(1)}, ≈€^{(2)}, ≈€^{(3)}}`. Combines Έ≤-10 (`≈€^+ ¬Ζ P_{i+1} = βàö((i+1)(N-i)) ¬Ζ E_{i, i+1}`) with Έ≤-27 (`P_k βàà adjoin`) and Έ≤-28 (`≈€^¬± βàà adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{≈€^{(Έ±)}}` (Έ≤-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(Έ±)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} βàà Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(Έ±)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is Έ≤-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ¬Ζ E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (Έ≤-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(Έ±)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to Έ≤-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is Έ≤-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is Έ≤-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (Έ≤-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **πüé· Tasaki ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `β³²^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1Χ², ≈€^{(1)}, ≈€^{(2)}, ≈€^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin β³² {≈€^{(1)}, ≈€^{(2)}, ≈€^{(3)}} = βäΛ`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} βàà adjoin` (Έ≤-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = βàë_{i,j} M_{i,j} βÄΔ E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (Έ≤-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `œÉ : Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `≈€_i^{(Έ±)}, ≈€_i^¬±`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Phase B-Έ≤ Έ≤-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

### Basis states and raising/lowering (Tasaki ¬ß2.1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.1 eqs. (2.1.4), (2.1.5), (2.1.6), p. 14.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body

case (`œÉ βàà {-1, 0, +1}`).

| `spinOnePiRot{1,2,3}_comm_*` | distinct-axis commutation `ΟΜ_Έ± ¬Ζ ΟΜ_Έ≤ = ΟΜ_Έ≤ ¬Ζ ΟΜ_Έ±` for integer S | `Quantum/SpinOneBasis.lean` |
| `spinOneRot{1,2,3}` | `Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ = 1 - i sin ΈΗ ¬Ζ ≈€^(Έ±) - (1 - cos ΈΗ) ¬Ζ (≈€^(Έ±))¬≤` (Tasaki Problem 2.1.c, all 3 axes) | `Quantum/SpinOneBasis.lean` |

(2.2.15)): `(1/4œÄ) βàΪβ²Ä^{2œÄ} dœÜ βàΪβ²Ä^œÄ dΈΗ sin ΈΗ ¬Ζ Ο¦^(3)_œÜ Ο¦^(2)_ΈΗ œ¹ (Ο¦^(3)_œÜ Ο¦^(2)_ΈΗ)βÄ† = (1/2) P_singlet` where `œ¹ = \|βÜëβ²¹βÜ™β²²βü©βü®βÜëβ²¹βÜ™β²²\|`. ### Time-reversal map for `S = 1/2` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.3 eqs. (2.3.4)βÄ™(2.3.8), pp. 26βÄ™27.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `timeReversalSpinHalf` | `Έ‰Χ²(v) = (-conj(v 1), conj(v 0))` (Tasaki eq. (2.3.6), `S = 1/2`); the antilinear unitary `ΟΜ_2 ¬Ζ KΧ²` of ¬ß2.3 | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `Έ‰Χ²|œà^βÜëβü© = |œà^βÜ™βü©` and `Έ‰Χ²|œà^βÜ™βü© = -|œà^βÜëβü©` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_add` | additivity `Έ‰Χ²(v + w) = Έ‰Χ²(v) + Έ‰Χ²(w)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_smul` | **antilinearity** in the scalar: `Έ‰Χ²(c βÄΔ v) = (conj c) βÄΔ Έ‰Χ²(v)` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 antilinearity warning, pp. 26βÄ™27) | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_sq` | **Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki eq. (2.3.8), half-odd-integer): `Έ‰Χ²¬≤ = -1Χ²` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | spin sign flip (Tasaki eq. (2.3.14)): `Έ‰Χ²(≈€^(Έ±) ¬Ζ v) = (-≈€^(Έ±)) ¬Ζ (Έ‰Χ² v)` for `Έ± = 1, 2, 3` βÄî equivariance form of `Έ‰Χ² ≈€^(Έ±) Έ‰Χ²β¹Μ¬Ι = -≈€^(Έ±)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf` | the antilinear complex-conjugation map `KΧ²` of Tasaki ¬ß2.3 eq. (2.3.4) at `S = 1/2`: `KΧ²(v) i := conj(v i)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf_sq` / `_add` / `_smul` | `KΧ²` is involutive (`KΧ²¬≤ = id`), additive, and antilinear in the scalar | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_eq_spinHalfRot2_pi_mulVec` | the **factorisation identity** of Tasaki ¬ß2.3: `Έ‰Χ² = ΟΜ_2 ¬Ζ KΧ²` where `ΟΜ_2 = spinHalfRot2 œÄ` is the œÄ rotation about the `2`-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `flipConfig` / `flipConfig_apply` / `flipConfig_involutive` | the spin-flip on a many-body configuration `œÉ : Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin 2`: `flipConfig œÉ x := 1 - œÉ x`; involutive | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign` / `timeReversalSign_zero` / `_one` / `_mul_flip` | per-site sign factor `ΈΒ(0) = 1`, `ΈΒ(1) = -1` for the multi-spin time-reversal, with `ΈΒ(s) ¬Ζ ΈΒ(1 - s) = -1` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` | multi-spin time-reversal map (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 lattice extension, `S = 1/2`): `(Έ‰Χ²_tot v) œ³ := (βàè_x ΈΒ(œ³ x)) ¬Ζ conj(v (flip œ³))` for finite `Έ¦` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `βàè_x ΈΒ(œ³ x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|Έ¦|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `Έ‰Χ²_tot¬≤ = (-1)^|Έ¦| ¬Ζ 1Χ²` βÄî `+1Χ²` when `|Έ¦|` is even, `-1Χ²` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec` | action of `Έ‰Χ²_tot` on a many-body basis state: `Έ‰Χ²_tot |Έ®_œÉβü© = (βàè_x ΈΒ(flip œÉ x)) ¬Ζ |Έ®_{flip œÉ}βü©` βÄî natural many-body generalisation of `Έ‰Χ²|βÜëβü© = |βÜ™βü©` and `Έ‰Χ²|βÜ™βü© = -|βÜëβü©` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliZ_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `œÉ^z` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) lifted to many-body): `Έ‰Χ²_tot ((onSite x œÉ^z) v) = (-(onSite x œÉ^z))(Έ‰Χ²_tot v)`. Diagonal-action case; `œÉ^x`, `œÉ^y` deferred | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `siteFlipAt` / `siteFlipAt_self` / `siteFlipAt_of_ne` / `flipConfig_siteFlipAt_comm` / `siteFlipAt_involutive` | per-site flip helpers: `siteFlipAt œ³ x` flips slot `x` only; commutes with `flipConfig`; involutive. The combinatorial primitive underlying off-diagonal `œÉ^x_x` / `œÉ^y_x` action (deferred) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x œÉ^x).mulVec |Έ®_œÉβü© = |Έ®_{siteFlipAt œÉ x}βü©` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x œÉ^x).mulVec v) œ³ = v (siteFlipAt œ³ x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `βàè_y ΈΒ((siteFlipAt œ³ x) y) = -(βàè_y ΈΒ(œ³ y))` βÄî the per-site flip swaps `ΈΒ(œ³ x)` with `ΈΒ(1 - œ³ x) = -ΈΒ(œ³ x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `œÉ^x` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) at Έ± = 1): `Έ‰Χ²_tot ((onSite x œÉ^x) v) = (-(onSite x œÉ^x))(Έ‰Χ²_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `œÉ^y` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) at Έ± = 2): `Έ‰Χ²_tot ((onSite x œÉ^y) v) = (-(onSite x œÉ^y))(Έ‰Χ²_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `¬±i` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `Έ‰Χ²_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`Έ‰Χ²_tot(c βÄΔ v) = conj(c) βÄΔ Έ‰Χ²_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) βÄî foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `≈€^(Έ±) = œÉ^(Έ±) / 2`: `Έ‰Χ²_tot ((onSite x ≈€^(Έ±)) v) = (-(onSite x ≈€^(Έ±)))(Έ‰Χ²_tot v)` for Έ± = 1, 2, 3 βÄî direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y`** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3): `Έ‰Χ²_tot ((≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y) v) = (≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y)(Έ‰Χ²_tot v)` βÄî two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `Έ‰Χ²_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(Έ‰Χ²_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + Έ‰Χ²_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : β³ù` makes `(-(J : β³²))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `Έ‰Χ²_tot` for any real coupling `J : β³ù`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `Έ‰Χ²_tot |βÜëβÜ™βü© = -|βÜ™βÜëβü©` and `Έ‰Χ²_tot |βÜ™βÜëβü© = -|βÜëβÜ™βü©` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|βÜëβÜ™βü© - |βÜ™βÜëβü©` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 / ¬ßA.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `Έ‰Χ²_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©` is **anti-invariant** under `Έ‰Χ²_tot`: `Έ‰Χ²_tot (|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©) = -(|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `Έ¦` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `Έ¦ = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp Έ¦` | `Matrix (Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin 2) (Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin 2) β³²` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : Έ¦` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian βÜ£ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x βâ† y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ¬Ζ onSite i B = onSite i (A ¬Ζ B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |

### Total spin operator (Tasaki ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.7), (2.2.8))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.2 eqs. (2.2.7) and (2.2.8), p. 22.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `totalSpinHalfOp1/2/3 Έ¦` | `≈€_tot^(Έ±) := ΈΘ_{x βàà Έ¦} onSite x ≈€^(Έ±)` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp1/2/3_isHermitian` | `≈€_tot^(Έ±)` is Hermitian | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfOp_onSite_comm_of_ne` | S = 1/2 named wrapper of `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_onSite_commutator_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}_onSite` | same-site commutator `[≈€_x^(Έ±), ≈€_x^(Έ≤)] = i ¬Ζ ≈€_x^(Έ≥)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus`, `totalSpinHalfOpMinus` | `≈€^¬±_tot := ΈΘ_{x βàà Έ¦} onSite x ≈€^¬±` (Tasaki (2.2.8)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_eq_add`, `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_eq_sub` | `≈€^¬±_tot = ≈€^(1)_tot ¬± i ¬Ζ ≈€^(2)_tot` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus_conjTranspose` | `(≈€^¬±_tot)βÄ† = ≈€^βà™_tot` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `[≈€_tot^(Έ±), ≈€_tot^(Έ≤)] = i ¬Ζ ≈€_tot^(Έ≥)` (total spin commutation) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState` | general-`K, L, M` 3D cubic checkerboard: `Έ‰Χ²_tot (neelCubicState K L M) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig K L M))` (no sign because `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`). | `basisVec_apply` / `basisVec_self` / `basisVec_of_ne` | foundational evaluation lemmas for the standard basis vectors: explicit `if`-form, diagonal `=1`, and off-diagonal `=0` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `sum_mul_basisVec` / `basisVec_sum_mul` | selector-sum identities `βàë œ³, f œ³ ¬Ζ basisVec œÉ œ³ = f œÉ` (and the symmetric form), the workhorses for inner-product computations on the spin Hilbert space | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec_inner` | basis-vector orthonormality `βàë œ³, basisVec œÉ œ³ ¬Ζ basisVec œ¹ œ³ = if œ¹ = œÉ then 1 else 0`. Real bilinear pairing (no complex conjugation needed since `basisVec` values are 0 or 1) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisSwap_ne_self` | `œÉ x βâ† œÉ y βÜ£ basisSwap œÉ x y βâ† œÉ` (the swap of an antiparallel pair changes the configuration). Useful for orthogonality computations on swapped states | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | the per-adjacent-bond expectation `βü®ΈΠ_NΟ©el, ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©elβü© = -1/4` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient at S = 1/2). Combines `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_adjacent` (bond action) with the orthogonality + norm¬≤ lemmas to compute `(1/2)¬Ζ0 - (1/4)¬Ζ1 = -1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic lemma: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `œÉ`, `βü®basisVec œÉ, ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y ¬Ζ basisVec œÉβü© = -1/4`. The 1-line foundation for every NΟ©el-bond expectation | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_parallel` | parallel companion: for `œÉ x = œÉ y` (and `x βâ† y`), `βü®basisVec œÉ, ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y ¬Ζ basisVec œÉβü© = +1/4`. Both basis vectors at the parallel pair are eigenvectors of `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` (eigenvalue `+1/4`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_parallel_eq_one_quarter` | NΟ©el chain same-sublattice (parallel) bond expectation `+1/4`: for any `x βâ† y` with `x.val % 2 = y.val % 2` (e.g., `(0, 2)`, `(1, 3)`), `βü®ΈΠ_NΟ©el, ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©elβü© = +1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOp3_mul_onSite_spinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `(≈€^(3)_x ¬Ζ ≈€^(3)_y) ¬Ζ basisVec œÉ = (spinHalfSign œÉ x ¬Ζ spinHalfSign œÉ y) ¬Ζ basisVec œÉ`: every basis vector is an eigenvector of the diagonal `≈€^z¬Ζ≈€^z` correlator. | `inner_basisVec_szsz_basisVec` | generic `βü®basisVec œÉ, ≈€^(3)_x ¬Ζ ≈€^(3)_y ¬Ζ basisVec œÉβü© = spinHalfSign œÉ x ¬Ζ spinHalfSign œÉ y`. The diagonal-only spin-spin correlator on a basis state | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfSign_mul_antiparallel` | for antiparallel `s βâ† t : Fin 2`, `spinHalfSign s ¬Ζ spinHalfSign t = -(1/4)`. Made public in PR #332 to power the generic `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel = -(1/4)` NΟ©el correlator | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `neelChainState_inner_off_diagonal_correlator_adjacent_eq_zero` | the per-adjacent-bond off-diagonal correlator on the NΟ©el chain vanishes: `βü®ΈΠ_NΟ©el, (≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y - ≈€^(3)_x ¬Ζ ≈€^(3)_y) ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©elβü© = 0`. Direct application of the generic helper | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_szsz_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | per-adjacent-bond `≈€^(3)_x ¬Ζ ≈€^(3)_y` correlation on the NΟ©el chain: `-1/4`. | `neelSquareState_inner_szsz_{horizontal,vertical}_{adjacent,wrap}_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 2D NΟ©el: per-bond `≈€^(3)¬Ζ≈€^(3)` correlation `-1/4` for every horizontal / vertical adjacent and wrap bond | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |

| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_wrap_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 1D wrap-bond expectation `-1/4` on the periodic NΟ©el chain `Fin (2(K+1))` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareState_inner_spinHalfDot_horizontal_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_vertical_adjacent_` / `_horizontal_wrap_` / `_vertical_wrap_` | 2D NΟ©el: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every horizontal / vertical adjacent and wrap bond | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicState_inner_spinHalfDot_{x,y,z}_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_{x,y,z}_wrap_` | 3D NΟ©el: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. | `neelChainState_energy_expectation_K1` | `K = 1` (2-site) open-chain Heisenberg energy expectation `βü®ΈΠ_NΟ©el, H_open ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©elβü© = J/2`. Combines `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` (`H = -2J ¬Ζ spinHalfDot 0 1`) with the per-bond `-1/4` expectation, giving `-2J ¬Ζ (-1/4) = J/2` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelConfigOf` / `neelStateOf` | generic graph-centric NΟ©el state from a sublattice indicator `A : V βÜ£ Bool`: `neelConfigOf A x := if A x then βÜë else βÜ™` and `neelStateOf A := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`. The chain / 2D / 3D `neelXyzConfig` / `neelXyzState` definitions are bridged via `_eq_neelConfigOf` / `_eq_neelStateOf`. Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.2) graph-centric form | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` action on the canonical NΟ©el state: for any `x βâ† y` with `A x βâ† A y`, `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A) = (1/2) ¬Ζ |swap_{x, y} ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü© - (1/4) ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)`. Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.3) graph-centric form. The chain / 2D / 3D `_adjacent` / `_wrap` bond actions are 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_spinHalfDot_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` expectation on the canonical NΟ©el state: for any `x βâ† y` with `A x βâ† A y`, `βü®ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A), ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü© = -(1/4)`. Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `≈€^z_x ¬Ζ ≈€^z_y` correlation on the canonical NΟ©el state: for any `A x βâ† A y`, `βü®ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A), ≈€^z_x ¬Ζ ≈€^z_y ¬Ζ ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü© = -(1/4)`. Diagonal half of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.4) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `βàè_{x βàà A} (-1)^(œÉ x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V βÜ£ Bool`, and configuration `œÉ : V βÜ£ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^βÜ™)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `βàè_{x even} (-1)^(œÉ x)`; specialisation to the NΟ©el configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `βàè_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(œÉ (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) Ο½ Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard NΟ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `βàè_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(œÉ ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) Ο½ Fin (2L)) Ο½ Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard NΟ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig œÉ) = (-1)^K ¬Ζ marshallSignChainConfig K œÉ`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)¬Ζ(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K œÉ βÄΔ basisVec œÉ`; specialisation at the NΟ©el configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the NΟ©el state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the NΟ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the NΟ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* NΟ©el configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) βÄî direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* NΟ©el state coincides with the time-reversed NΟ©el state in 1D, 2D, 3D βÄî both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. Establishes a direct bridge between the Marshall basis change (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 / Marshall-Lieb-Mattis) and the time-reversal operator (Tasaki ¬ß2.3) on the NΟ©el ground-state ansatz | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis A œÉ` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A œÉ βÄΔ basisVec œÉ` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V βÜ£ Bool` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `βâΛ 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the PerronβÄ™Frobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `Έ®ΧÉ^œÉ œÉ = marshallSignOf A œÉ`; `Έ®ΧÉ^œÉ œ³ = 0` for `œ³ βâ† œÉ`; explicit `Έ®ΧÉ^œÉ œ³ = marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬Ζ basisVec œÉ œ³` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `¬±1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A œÉ)¬≤ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `ΈΘ_œ³ Έ®ΧÉ^œÉ œ³ ¬Ζ Έ®ΧÉ^œ¹ œ³ = if œ¹ = œÉ then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{œÉΧ³/2}` as the underlying `basisVec œÉ` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `ΈΘ_x œÉ_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) œÉ œÉ').im = 0` for all `x, y, œÉ, œÉ'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the MarshallβÄ™LiebβÄ™Mattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : Έ¦ βÜ£ Έ¦ βÜ£ β³²` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ΈΘ_{x,y} J(x,y) ¬Ζ spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) œÉ œÉ').im = 0`. β³ù-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A œÉ` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A œÉ).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `¬±1 βàà β³ù` (either `1` or `(-1 : β³²)^(œÉ x : β³ï)` with `(œÉ x : β³ï) βàà {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `ΈΘ_œ³ \|Έ®ΧÉ^œÉβü© œ³ ¬Ζ (H ¬Ζ \|Έ®ΧÉ^{œÉ'}βü©) œ³` is real (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). Reduces to `marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬Ζ marshallSignOf A œÉ' ¬Ζ H œÉ œÉ'` (each factor real) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `ΈΘ_œ³ \|Έ®ΧÉ^œÉβü© œ³ ¬Ζ (M ¬Ζ \|Έ®ΧÉ^{œÉ'}βü©) œ³ = marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬Ζ marshallSignOf A œÉ' ¬Ζ M œÉ œÉ'`. Generalises the inner-product computation used in Property (i) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x βâ† A y`) with `œÉ` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`œÉ x βâ† œÉ y`), `marshallSignOf A œÉ * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap œÉ x y) = -1`. The combined product over `Έ¦` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(¬±1)¬≤ = 1`; at the unique site in `A βà© {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(œÉ x + œÉ y) = -1` since `œÉ x βâ† œÉ y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `œÉ βâ† œÉ'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬Ζ marshallSignOf A œÉ' ¬Ζ J(x,y) ¬Ζ (spinHalfDot x y) œÉ œÉ'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. Case analysis on `(spinHalfDot x y) œÉ œÉ'` (zero off-diagonal except at `œÉ = basisSwap œÉ' x y`, antiparallel œÉ', `x βâ† y`) combined with the Marshall sign relation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `œÉ βâ† œÉ'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `ΈΘ_œ³ \|Έ®ΧÉ^œÉβü© œ³ ¬Ζ (H ¬Ζ \|Έ®ΧÉ^{œÉ'}βü©) œ³` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. The **Marshall sign trick** at the heart of the MarshallβÄ™LiebβÄ™Mattis Theorem 2.2 proof | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `œÉ βÜΠ basisSwap œÉ x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `œÉ x βâ† œÉ y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `œÉ x βâ† œÉ y`, `SwapReachable G œÉ (basisSwap œÉ x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `œÉ z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `œÉ x βâ† œÉ y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 41) βÄî combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronβÄ™Frobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `Hβ²ÄIndex Έ¦` | index type `{œÉ : Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin 2 // magnetization Έ¦ œÉ = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `Hβ²ÄIndex Έ¦` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬Ζ marshallSignOf A œ³ ¬Ζ (H_J)_{œÉ,œ³})` βÄî the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronβÄ™Frobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `œÉ βâ† œ³`, packaged from PR Έ±-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap œÉ x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap œÉ x y = œÉ βà‰ Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `Έ¦`); the magnetisation `βàë_z spinSign(œÉ z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). Key ingredient for Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 42 Proposition (equal-magnetisation reachability) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetization.lean` |
| `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `œÉ` and `œÉ'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `œÉ βâ† œÉ'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `œÉ x = 0, œÉ' x = 1`) and `y` (with `œÉ y = 1, œÉ' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `œÉ βâ† œÉ'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x βàà D01`, `y βàà D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `œÉ'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `œÉ'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `œÉ`, `œÉ'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `βâΞ 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronβÄ™Frobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c¬ΖI βà£ M` on `Hβ²ÄIndex Έ¦`. Used as input to PerronβÄ™Frobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c βâΞ M œÉ œÉ` for all `œÉ`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) œÉ (basisSwap œÉ x y) = 1/2` for `x βâ† y` and antiparallel `œÉ_x βâ† œÉ_y`. Building block for the explicit Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations needed for PerronβÄ™Frobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotSwapEntry.lean` |
| `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x βâ† y`, `œÉ_x βâ† œÉ_y`, and `(u, v) βàâ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap œÉ x y) u v βâ† œÉ`. Site analysis: `œÉ` and `œÉ' = basisSwap œÉ x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `œÉ`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. Used for off-bond vanishing in the Heisenberg matrix entry computation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapEntry.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `œÉ' = basisSwap œÉ x y` (with `x βâ† y`, `œÉ_x βâ† œÉ_y`) and any `(u, v) βàâ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) œÉ œÉ' = 0`. Three cases: `u = v` (diagonal), `u βâ† v` parallel œÉ' (constant action), `u βâ† v` antiparallel + off-bond (combinatorial helper) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotOffBond.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) œÉ (basisSwap œÉ x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. Decomposes the double sum and uses Έ±-5e (active bond = 1/2) + Έ±-5g (off-bond = 0). For symmetric `J`, simplifies to `J x y` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick Ο½ Heisenberg formula Ο½ symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. Combined with `J(x, y).re > 0` on graph edges gives strict negativity of M off-diagonal at swap pairs, hence strict positivity of `B = c¬ΖI βà£ M` βÄî the input for PerronβÄ™Frobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/DressedSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B œÉ œ³` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. Combines the dressed matrix value `-J(x, y).re` (PR Έ±-5i) with the off-diagonal definition `B œÉ œ³ = -M œÉ œ³` (PR Έ±-5d). Single-step strict positivity for PerronβÄ™Frobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (Έ±-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) œÉ œ³ > 0` and `B œ³ œ¹ > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) œÉ œ¹ > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `œÉ : Hβ²ÄIndex Έ¦` and any `ΈΨ` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) œÉ.val ΈΨ`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) œÉ βü®ΈΨ, h_magβü© > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using Έ±-5j (single-step swap positivity) and Έ±-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). Key bridge from combinatorial reachability to PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ¬Ζ I βà£ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M œÉ œÉ` (strict). Cases on `œÉ = œ³` (use diagonal positivity) vs `œÉ βâ† œ³` (use Έ±-5c reachability + Έ±-5m matrix-power lift). Final input for PerronβÄ™Frobenius application | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedIrreducible.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR Έ±-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronβÄ™Frobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **PerronβÄ™Frobenius applied to `B = c ¬Ζ I βà£ M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `ΈΦ`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. Translating back to `M`, `v` is the eigenvector for the **minimum** eigenvalue (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg). This is the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4): the H_0 ground-state expansion `ΈΘ_œÉ c_œÉ \|Έ®ΧÉ^œÉβü©` with `c_œÉ = v œÉ > 0` is unique up to positive scalar | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `bipartiteCoupling` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian` | the Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 40 toy Hamiltonian setup: `bipartiteCoupling A x y := if A x βâ† A y then 1 else 0` (the unnormalised bipartite coupling), and `heisenbergToyHamiltonian A := heisenbergHamiltonian (bipartiteCoupling A)`. Real symmetric, non-negative, supported on bipartite bonds, positive on inter-sublattice pairs. Hermitian. Used in subsequent PRs to derive `S_tot = 0` for the AFM Heisenberg ground state via inner-product comparison | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonian.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `Έ¦` from sublattice indicator `A : Έ¦ βÜ£ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x βâ† A y`. The natural bond graph for the toy Hamiltonian (every edge of `bipartiteCoupling A` is a `bipartiteGraphFromA A`-edge and vice versa) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. Cases on `A x = A y` (length-2 path via opposite sublattice) vs `A x βâ† A y` (direct edge). Provides the `G.Preconnected` hypothesis needed for MLM application to the toy Hamiltonian | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_toy_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) for the toy Hamiltonian**: the shifted toy matrix `B_toy = c ¬Ζ I βà£ M_toy` (under both-sublattices-nonempty + diagonal-shift hypothesis) has a unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive eigenvector. Specialises Έ±-5o to the toy via Έ±-6a + Έ±-6b | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyPF.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp1/2/3` | sublattice spin operators `≈€_A^(Έ±) := ΈΘ_{x βàà A} onSite x ≈€^(Έ±)` for `Έ± βàà {1, 2, 3}`. Foundation for the Casimir identity `ΡΛ_toy = (1/(2|Έ¦|))((≈€_tot)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_A)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_B)¬≤)` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp1/2/3` / `totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_sublattice_sum` | **Spin-`S` analogues**: `≈€_A^(Έ±) := ΈΘ_{x : A x} onSiteS x (spinSOp_Έ± N)` for `Έ± βàà {1, 2, 3}`, with decomposition `≈€_tot^(Έ±) = ≈€_A^(Έ±) + ≈€_¬§A^(Έ±)`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | Spin-`S` sublattice operator Hermiticity (Έ≥-4 step 2). Sum of Hermitian summands is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1043) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_isHermitian` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir `(≈€_A)¬≤ := ΈΘ_Έ± (≈€_A^(Έ±))¬≤` plus Hermiticity. Foundation for the Casimir identity in Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11) at general spin-S (Έ≥-4 step 3) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1044) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice same-axis commutativity `Commute (≈€_A^(Έ±)) (≈€_¬§A^(Έ±))` for Έ± βàà {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (≈€_A^(Έ±)) (≈€_¬§A^(Έ≤))` for any Έ±, Έ≤ βàà {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_cross_commute` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir cross-commute: `Commute (≈€_A)¬≤ (≈€_¬§A)¬≤`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{2,3,1}` | spin-`S` sublattice SU(2) algebra: `[≈€_A^Έ±, ≈€_A^Έ≤] = i ΈΒ^Έ±Έ≤Έ≥ ≈€_A^Έ≥`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total Casimir: `Commute (≈€_A)¬≤ (≈€_tot)¬≤`. Direct ring computation from PR #2779's simplified form via `(¬§¬§A) = A` at the filter level (PR #2780, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyComplement.lean`) |

Three eigenvector witnesses: `(≈€_tot)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|œÉ_βäΛβü© = s_A(s_A+1) ¬Ζ \|œÉ_βäΛβü©` (PR #878 with `\|V\| = \|A\|`), `(≈€_A)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|œÉ_βäΛβü© = s_A(s_A+1) ¬Ζ \|œÉ_βäΛβü©` (PR #1067), `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|œÉ_βäΛβü© = 0 ¬Ζ \|œÉ_βäΛβü©` (PR #1067 with `\|¬§A\| = 0` collapsing the eigenvalue formula) (PR #2782, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGroundStateSaturated.lean`) |

Together with PR #2782, gives two distinct elements of the predicted GS subspace (for `N βâΞ 1`, where `\|œÉ_βäΛβü© βâ† \|œÉ_βäΞβü©`; for `N = 0` the spin-`0` case collapses to a 1-dim space) βÄî and via the saturated-ferromagnet ladder, the entire (2 s_A + 1)-dim irrep is contained for any `N` (PR #2783, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGroundStateSaturatedLast.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_of_cardNotA_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_totalSpinSSquared_of_cardNotA_zero` | **sublattice βÜî total spin identities at the saturated edge case**: when `\|¬§A\| = 0`, the complement sublattice `¬§A` is empty, so `≈€_¬§A^(Έ±) = 0` and `≈€_¬§A¬≤ = 0`, and the `A`-sublattice operators coincide with the total operators: `≈€_A^(Έ±) = ≈€_tot^(Έ±)` for `Έ± βàà {1,2,3}` and `≈€_A¬≤ = ≈€_tot¬≤`. Foundation for the dim-bound on the predicted GS subspace in the saturated case: `eigenspace(≈€_tot¬≤)_Έ± βäÜ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` whenever `\|¬§A\| = 0` and `Έ± = s_A(s_A+1)` (PR #2784, file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeEqTotalOfCardZero.lean`) |
