Proof: any `(≈€_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector at `s_A(s_A+1)` automatically satisfies all three joint Casimir eigenspace conditions, since (i) `(≈€_tot)¬≤` eigenvalue matches the predicted target `(s_Aβà£0)(s_Aβà£0+1)`, (ii) `≈€_A¬≤ = ≈€_tot¬≤` (PR #2784) gives the `(≈€_A)¬≤` condition for free, (iii) `≈€_¬§A¬≤ = 0` trivially. Combined with PR #2768's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace = span(ladderIterateUp)` and finrank `2 m_max + 1`, this yields the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS) βâΞ 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` at the saturated edge case, matching the Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction `2 S_tot + 1` (PR #2785, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSEigenspaceBridge.lean`) |

The complement orientation has the opposite sign of the original orientation's signed sublattice imbalance. Building block for Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3's orientation-independent total-spin magnitude `\|\|A\| βà£ \|¬§A\|\|¬ΖS` (PR #2841, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_complement_norm_eq` | **orientation independence of the imbalance-weight norm**: `βÄ•bipartiteImbalanceWeight (¬§A) NβÄ• = βÄ•bipartiteImbalanceWeight A NβÄ•`. Direct corollary of PR #2841 (`_complement_eq_neg`) and `norm_neg`. Confirms the orientation-independent total-spin magnitude `\|\|A\| βà£ \|¬§A\|\|¬ΖS` predicted by Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 (PR #2842, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq_mMax_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **imbalance-weight norm at saturated edges = `|Έ¦|¬ΖN/2`**: at `\|¬§A\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `βÄ•bipartiteImbalanceWeight A NβÄ• = \|Έ¦\|¬ΖN/2 = m_max`. Matches the maximum total spin attained at the all-aligned ferromagnetic state (PR #2850, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_eq_zero_iff_card_eq` | **characterisation of `bipartiteImbalanceWeight = 0`**: at `N βâΞ 1`, `bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N = 0 βÜî \|A\| = \|¬§A\|`. Strengthens PR #2773's forward direction with the converse via the real-part formula and `N/2 > 0` (PR #2854, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightEqZeroIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq_zero_iff_card_eq` | **norm-zero characterisation**: at `N βâΞ 1`, `βÄ•bipartiteImbalanceWeight A NβÄ• = 0 βÜî \|A\| = \|¬§A\|`. Direct from `norm_eq_zero` + PR #2854 (PR #2855, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormEqZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_pos_of_cardA_gt_cardNotA_of_N_pos` / `_re_neg_of_cardA_lt_cardNotA_of_N_pos` | **strict sign of the imbalance-weight real part** (at `N βâΞ 1`): `\|A\| > \|¬§A\|` gives `(bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N).re > 0`; `\|A\| < \|¬§A\|` gives `< 0`. | `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_norm_eq` | **NΟ©el-state magnetization norm = Theorem 2.3 predicted spin**: `βÄ•magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)βÄ• = \|\|A\| βà£ \|¬§A\|\|¬ΖN/2 = \|\|A\| βà£ \|¬§A\|\|¬ΖS`. Direct from PR #2773's bridge + PR #2826's norm formula. Quantitative connection from the NΟ©el variational input to Theorem 2.3's predicted spin (PR #2857, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateMagNormEqPredicted.lean`) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_norm_eq` | **complement NΟ©el-state magnetization norm = same predicted spin**: `βÄ•magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (¬§A) N)βÄ• = \|\|A\| βà£ \|¬§A\|\|¬ΖN/2`. The complement orientation has the negated magnetization eigenvalue, so its norm is the same. Confirms orientation independence of Theorem 2.3 (PR #2858, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateComplementMagNormEqPredicted.lean`) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_norm_eq_mMax_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **NΟ©el-state mag norm at saturated edges = `m_max`**: at `\|¬§A\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `βÄ•magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)βÄ• = \|Έ¦\|¬ΖN/2 = m_max`. Positive sign reflects parallel alignment within each sublattice. Companion to Έ≥-4 step 157; relies on the parallel-diagonal lemmas `spinSDot_apply_diag_neelConfigOfS_of_same` and `spinHalfDot_apply_diag_neelConfigOf_of_same` (the latter using a new `spinHalfDot_apply_diag_of_ne_parallel`) (Έ≥-4 step 158) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1205) |
| `neelStateOfS_expectation_spinSDot_self` / `neelStateOf_expectation_spinHalfDot_self` | same-site (diagonal) per-pair NΟ©el correlation: `<ΈΠ_NΟ©el \| ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_x \| ΈΠ_NΟ©el> = N(N+2)/4` (spin-S) and `3/4` (spin-1/2). Universal local Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)`, completing the cross/same/self trio of per-pair correlations. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Έ≥-4 step 235 (Έ≥-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(≈€^1)¬≤+(≈€^2)¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on NΟ©el**: equal expectations on `ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)` and `ΈΠ_NΟ©el(¬§A)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|¬ΖN/2` via Έ≥-4 step 220 + 221 (Έ≥-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on NΟ©el**: both expectations equal `\|Έ¦\|/2`. Direct from Έ≥-4 step 219 (œÉ-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of Έ≥-4 step 237 (Έ≥-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `≈€_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on NΟ©el**: `<ΈΠ_NΟ©el(¬§A)\|≈€_tot^(3)\|ΈΠ_NΟ©el(¬§A)> = βà£<ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)\|≈€_tot^(3)\|ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)>`. Direct from Έ≥-4 step 206 + 231 (Έ≥-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `≈€_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on NΟ©el**. Direct from Έ≥-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Έ≥-4 step 239 (Έ≥-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` NΟ©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<ΈΠ_NΟ©el\|≈€_tot^(1,2)\|ΈΠ_NΟ©el> = 0`. Trivial corollary of Έ≥-4 step 214 applied to `œÉ = neelConfigOfS A N` (Έ≥-4 step 241). Together with Έ≥-4 step 91 (`<≈€_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|βà£\|¬§A\|)¬ΖN/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` NΟ©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as Έ≥-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from Έ≥-4 step 215 (Έ≥-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ΈΘ_{j=1}^z ≈€_0 ¬Ζ ≈€_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `βà£S(1 + zS)` (Έ≥-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j βàà univ.erase 0` (Έ≥-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = βàÖ` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `βà£S(1 + zS)` is intended for `z βâΞ 1` (Έ≥-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<ΈΠ_βäΛ\|H\|ΈΠ_βäΛ> = z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `βà£S(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (Έ≥-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<ΈΠ_aligned(c)\|H\|ΈΠ_aligned(c)> = z¬Ζ(N/2 βà£ c.val)¬≤`. Generalises Έ≥-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤` (Έ≥-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<ΈΠ_βäΞ\|H\|ΈΠ_βäΞ> = z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤`. Direct specialisation of Έ≥-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (Έ≥-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `≈€_R^(Έ±) = ΈΘ_{j βâ† 0} onSiteS j ≈€^(Έ±)` for axes `Έ± = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `≈€^(Έ±)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (≈€_tot¬≤ βà£ ≈€_0¬≤ βà£ ≈€_R¬≤)/2` (Έ≥-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`≈€_0 ¬Ζ ≈€_R` decomposition**: `H = ΈΘ_Έ± onSiteS 0 (≈€^(Έ±)) ¬Ζ leafSpinSOp_Έ±`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `ΈΘ_j (A * B_j) = A * (ΈΘ_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (Έ≥-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_Έ± (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (≈€^(Έ±)) + leafSpinSOp_Έ± z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (Έ≥-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `≈€_R¬≤ := (≈€_R^(1))¬≤ + (≈€_R^(2))¬≤ + (≈€_R^(3))¬≤`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (Έ≥-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 ≈€^(Έ±)) (leafSpinSOp_Έ± z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (Έ≥-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2¬ΖH = (≈€_tot)¬≤ βà£ ≈€_0¬≤ βà£ ≈€_R¬≤` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `≈€_0¬≤ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `≈€_R¬≤ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `ΈΘ_Έ± totalSpinSOp_Έ±¬≤ = ΈΘ_Έ± (onSite 0 + leaf_Έ±)¬≤` via Έ≥-5 step 251 + `(a+b)¬≤ = a¬≤ + 2ab + b¬≤` (using Έ≥-5 step 253 commutativity) + Έ≥-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (Έ≥-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **β³²-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : β³²) βÄΔ H = (≈€_tot)¬≤ βà£ ≈€_0¬≤ βà£ ≈€_R¬≤`. Direct corollary of Έ≥-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (Έ≥-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 ¬Ζ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot¬≤\|v> βà£ <v\|S0¬≤\|v> βà£ <v\|SR¬≤\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of Έ≥-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (Έ≥-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) ¬Ζ <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0¬≤` term in the Casimir decomposition (Έ≥-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 ¬Ζ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot¬≤\|v> βà£ (N(N+2)/4)¬Ζ<v\|v> βà£ <v\|SR¬≤\|v>`. Combines Έ≥-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0¬≤` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (Έ≥-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `Stot¬≤¬Ζv = Έ±¬Ζv` and `SR¬≤¬Ζv = Έ≤¬Ζv`, then `H¬Ζv = (Έ± βà£ N(N+2)/4 βà£ Έ≤)/2 ¬Ζ v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(Έ±, Έ≤)` joint eigenvalues (Έ≥-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N ¬Ζ v = (N(N+2)/4) βÄΔ v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from Έ≥-5 step 259 (Έ≥-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_Έ± 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = βàÖ` in `Fin 1` (Έ≥-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = ΈΘ_{j,k βàà univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (Έ≥-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<ΈΠ_βäΛ\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|ΈΠ_βäΛ> = (zN/2)¬Ζ(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = z¬Ζ(N/2)` is max leaf spin. Computed by rearranging Έ≥-5 step 254 (Casimir decomposition) and applying closed forms for `<Stot¬≤>`, `<S0¬≤>`, `<H>` (Έ≥-5 step 263) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1310) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form on allUp**: `H ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΛβü© = z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©`. Proof: each leaf-pair `spinSDot 0 j` at `j βâ† 0` gives `(N¬≤/4)¬Ζ\|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©` (via `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne`); sum over `z` leaves (Έ≥-5 step 264) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1311) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form** of `≈€_R¬≤` on allUp: `≈€_R¬≤ ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΛβü© = (zN/2)¬Ζ(zN/2+1) ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©`. Witnesses that `\|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©` is in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`. Combined with Έ≥-5 step 264 + existing `Stot¬≤` eigenvector identity, confirms `\|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©` is a joint eigenstate of `H, Stot¬≤, ≈€_0¬≤, ≈€_R¬≤`. Proof: rearrange Έ≥-5 step 255 + apply (Έ≥-5 step 265) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1312) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form on allDown**: `H ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΞβü© = z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΞβü©`. AllDown mirror of Έ≥-5 step 264; same eigenvalue (spin-flip symmetry) (Έ≥-5 step 266) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1313) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form** of `≈€_R¬≤` on allDown: `≈€_R¬≤ ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΞβü© = (zN/2)¬Ζ(zN/2+1) ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_βäΞβü©`. AllDown mirror of Έ≥-5 step 265; both `\|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©` and `\|ΈΠ_βäΞβü©` lie in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`, differing only by total `≈€_tot^(3)` magnetization (Έ≥-5 step 267) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1314) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_gs_casimir_sector` | **GS-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `≈€_tot¬≤` at `((zβà£1)N/2)((zβà£1)N/2+1)` and `≈€_R¬≤` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ¬Ζ v = βà£(N/2)¬Ζ(zN/2+1) ¬Ζ v = βà£S(1+zS) ¬Ζ v`. Specialization of Έ≥-5 step 259 to the ground-state sector predicted by Tasaki Problem 2.5.a (Έ≥-5 step 268) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1315) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_max_casimir_sector` | **Max-Casimir-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `≈€_tot¬≤` at `((z+1)N/2)((z+1)N/2+1)` and `≈€_R¬≤` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ¬Ζ v = z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤ ¬Ζ v = zS¬≤ ¬Ζ v`. Maximum Casimir sector containing `\|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©, \|ΈΠ_βäΞβü©` (Έ≥-5 step 269) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1316) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_gs_energy_smul` | **Predicted ground-state energy** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a target): `singleClusterGSEnergyS z N := βà£(N/2)¬Ζ(zN/2+1) = βà£S(1+zS)`, with named eigenvalue identity `H ¬Ζ v = singleClusterGSEnergyS z N βÄΔ v` at the GS Casimir sector (Έ≥-5 step 270) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1317) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_max_energy_smul` | **Maximum Casimir-sector energy**: `singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N := z¬Ζ(N/2)¬≤ = zS¬≤`, with named eigenvalue identity `H ¬Ζ v = singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N βÄΔ v` at the max Casimir sector containing `\|ΈΠ_βäΛβü©, \|ΈΠ_βäΞβü©` (Έ≥-5 step 271) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1318) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_nonneg` | **Energy real-part sign hygiene**: `Re(GS) βâΛ 0` (AFM physical bound) and `0 βâΛ Re(Max)` (extremal aligned-state eigenvalue is non-negative) (Έ≥-5 step 272) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1319) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re` | **Energy ordering**: `Re(GS) βâΛ Re(Max)`. Consistency check between Έ≥-5 steps 268 and 269 (Έ≥-5 step 273) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1320) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_im_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_im_zero` | **Energies are real**: imaginary parts of both named single-cluster energies vanish (Έ≥-5 step 274) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1321) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_one_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_one_eq` | **Dimer (z=1) closed forms**: `singleClusterGSEnergyS 1 N = βà£(N/2)(N/2+1) = βà£S(S+1)` (canonical singlet eigenvalue of `≈€_0¬Ζ≈€_1`); `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 1 N = (N/2)¬≤ = S¬≤` (canonical triplet eigenvalue) (Έ≥-5 step 275) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1322) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_left` | **Trivial edge cases**: `GS z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max 0 N = 0` (single-site, no leaves) (Έ≥-5 step 276) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1323) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_eq` | **Explicit `.re` forms** as `β³ù` values: `Re(GS) = -(N/2)(zN/2+1)`, `Re(Max) = z¬ΖN¬≤/4`. Useful for downstream real comparisons (Έ≥-5 step 278) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1325) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_sub_singleClusterGSEnergyS` | **GS-Max energy gap**: `Max βà£ GS = (N/2)(zN+1) = S(2zS+1)`. Closed-form difference between the two named eigenvalues (Έ≥-5 step 280) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1327) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_lt_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_of_pos` | **Strict gap for `N βâΞ 1`**: `Re(GS) < Re(Max)` whenever spin is non-trivial (Έ≥-5 step 281) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1328) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_neg_of_pos` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_pos_of_pos` | **Strict sign**: `Re(GS) < 0` for `N βâΞ 1`; `0 < Re(Max)` for `z βâΞ 1, N βâΞ 1`. Strengthens Έ≥-5 step 272 (Έ≥-5 step 283) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1330) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_one` | **Single-leaf reduction**: `leafSpinSSquared 1 N = spinSDot 1 1 N` on `Fin 2`. Single-leaf leaf-Casimir collapses to single-site Casimir (Έ≥-5 step 285) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1332) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_one_mulVec` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer` | **Dimer eigenvalue from Stot¬≤ alone**: at z=1, `leafSpinSSquared` acts as the scalar `N(N+2)/4`, so any Stot¬≤ eigenvector is auto a joint eigenvector with `H ¬Ζ v = ((Έ± βà£ N(N+2)/2) / 2) βÄΔ v` (Έ≥-5 step 286) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1333) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer_singlet` | **Dimer singlet attains GS energy**: for z=1, any `Stot¬≤ = 0` eigenvector is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterGSEnergyS 1 N`. Strongest concrete realisation of Tasaki Problem 2.5.a in the dimer case (Έ≥-5 step 287) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1334) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer_top` | **Dimer top-spin attains Max energy**: for z=1, any `Stot¬≤ = N(N+1)` eigenvector (`s_tot = 2S`) is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 1 N` (Έ≥-5 step 288) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1335) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_two` | **Trimer (z=2) leaf-Casimir decomposition**: `leafSpinSSquared 2 N = (N(N+2)/2) βÄΔ 1 + 2 βÄΔ spinSDot 1 2 N` on `Fin 3`. Two diagonal `spinSDot j j` terms collapse to `N(N+2)/4 βÄΔ 1` each; off-diagonal `spinSDot 1 2`, `spinSDot 2 1` are equal by `spinSDot_comm` (Έ≥-5 step 292) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1339) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer` | **Trimer eigenvalue from `Stot¬≤` + leaf-leaf coupling**: for z=2, joint eigenvector of `Stot¬≤ (=Έ±)` and `spinSDot 1 2 (=Έ≤)` is an `H`-eigenvector at `(Έ± βà£ 3¬ΖN(N+2)/4 βà£ 2Έ≤)/2`. Specialisation of Έ≥-5 step 259 using Έ≥-5 step 292 (Έ≥-5 step 293) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1340) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer_gs` | **Trimer GS-sector eigenvalue at GS energy**: for z=2, joint eigenvector of `Stot¬≤¬Ζv=(N(N+2)/4)¬Ζv` (`s_tot=N/2`) and `spinSDot 1 2¬Ζv=(N¬≤/4)¬Ζv` (leaf triplet) is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterGSEnergyS 2 N = -N(N+1)/2` (Έ≥-5 step 294) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1341) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer_top` | **Trimer top-spin sector eigenvalue at Max energy**: for z=2, joint eigenvector at `s_tot=3N/2` (max) and leaf triplet `s_R=N` is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 2 N = N¬≤/2` (Έ≥-5 step 295) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1342) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer_leaf_singlet` | **Trimer leaf-singlet sector eigenvalue = 0**: for z=2, joint eigenvector at `s_tot=N/2` and leaf singlet `s_R=0` (`spinSDot 1 2¬Ζv = -(N(N+2)/4)¬Ζv`) gives `H ¬Ζ v = 0`. Decoupling sector containing `0` from spin-1/2 trimer spectrum `{-1, 0, 1/2}` (Έ≥-5 step 296) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1343) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_three` | **Quartet (z=3) leaf-Casimir decomposition**: `leafSpinSSquared 3 N = (3¬ΖN(N+2)/4) βÄΔ 1 + 2 βÄΔ (spinSDot 1 2 + spinSDot 1 3 + spinSDot 2 3)` on `Fin 4`. | `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_isHermitian` | sublattice spin Casimir: `(≈€_A)¬≤ := ΈΘ_Έ± (≈€_A^(Έ±))¬≤`. Hermitian (each `(≈€_A^(Έ±))¬≤` is the square of a Hermitian operator). Foundation for the Casimir identity `ΡΛ_toy = (1/(2|Έ¦|))((≈€_tot)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_A)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_B)¬≤)` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (≈€_A^(Έ±)) (≈€_¬§A^(Έ≤))` for any axes `Έ±, Έ≤ βàà {1, 2, 3}`. Generic helper expresses this for arbitrary single-site operators `S, T`; the six mixed-axis specialisations follow as one-line corollaries | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_cross_commute` | the two sublattice Casimirs commute: `Commute (≈€_A)¬≤ (≈€_¬§A)¬≤`. Each pairwise component `Commute ((≈€_A^(Έ±))¬≤) ((≈€_¬§A^(Έ≤))¬≤)` follows from the mixed-axes cross-commute by chaining `Commute.mul_left` / `mul_right`. Sets up the joint eigenbasis of `(≈€_tot)¬≤`, `(≈€_A)¬≤`, `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤` for the toy-Hamiltonian eigenvalue analysis | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | **Sublattice SU(2) algebra**: `[≈€_A^(Έ±), ≈€_A^(Έ≤)] = i ΈΒ^Έ±Έ≤Έ≥ ≈€_A^(Έ≥)` for each cyclic axis triple. Generic helper `sublatticeSpin_commutator_general` lifts the single-site commutator `[SΈ±, SΈ≤] = i βÄΔ SΈ≥` to the sublattice sum (off-diagonal pairs vanish by `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne`; diagonal contributes `if A x then i βÄΔ onSite x SΈ≥ else 0`) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | **Sublattice Casimir self-invariance**: `Commute (≈€_A)¬≤ (≈€_A^(Έ±))` for each axis. Standard SU(2) Casimir argument applied at the sublattice level: per-axis Leibniz rule `[X¬≤, C] = X[X,C] + [X,C]X` combined with the sublattice SU(2) algebra. Together with cross-commute, gives `Commute (≈€_A)¬≤ (≈€_tot^(Έ±))`, hence `Commute (≈€_A)¬≤ (≈€_tot)¬≤` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_complement` / `_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `(≈€_A)¬≤` commutes with each `≈€_¬§A^(Έ±)` (`Commute.mul_left` over the mixed-axes cross-commute), with each `≈€_tot^(Έ±) = ≈€_A^(Έ±) + ≈€_¬§A^(Έ±)`, and with `(≈€_tot)¬≤ = ΈΘ_Έ± (≈€_tot^(Έ±))¬≤`. Provides the **third pairwise commutativity** needed for the joint eigenbasis of `(≈€_tot)¬≤`, `(≈€_A)¬≤`, `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤` (the first two are Έ±-6r self-invariance and Έ±-6o inter-Casimir cross-commute) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot` / `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_isHermitian` | cross-sublattice spin dot product: `≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_B := ΈΘ_Έ± ≈€_A^(Έ±) ≈€_B^(Έ±)`. `≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_¬§A` is Hermitian (each summand is the product of two commuting Hermitian operators). Bilinear expansion `sublatticeSpinDot_eq_sum_sum`: `≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_B = ΈΘ_{x : A x} ΈΘ_{y : B y} ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` connects the operator-level Casimir form to the bond-level Heisenberg expression in the toy Hamiltonian (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.10)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` / `_of_const_on` | `(≈€_A)¬≤ = ΈΘ_{x βàà A} ΈΘ_{y βàà A} ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` (specialisation `B = A` of the bilinear expansion), and the **maximum-spin Casimir eigenvalue on the all-aligned state**: `(≈€_A)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|s s βÄΠ sβü© = (\|A\|¬Ζ(\|A\|+2)/4) ¬Ζ \|s s βÄΠ sβü©` for any `s : Fin 2`. Generalised form `_of_const_on`: any `\|œÉβü©` with `œÉ` **constant on `A`** is an eigenvector with eigenvalue `\|A\|¬Ζ(\|A\|+2)/4` (relevant for NΟ©el-style states which are constant on each sublattice but not globally) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_sublatticeSpinDot_sum` | the MLM toy Hamiltonian decomposes as an oriented cross-sublattice spin dot product: `ΡΛ_toy = ≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_¬§A + ≈€_¬§A ¬Ζ ≈€_A`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_two_sublatticeSpinDot` | cross-sublattice symmetry: `≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_¬§A = ≈€_¬§A ¬Ζ ≈€_A` (each axis pair commutes by `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute`), giving the closed form `ΡΛ_toy = 2 βÄΔ ≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_¬§A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir identity** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)): `(≈€_tot)¬≤ = (≈€_A)¬≤ + 2 βÄΔ (≈€_A ¬Ζ ≈€_¬§A) + (≈€_¬§A)¬≤` (per-axis `(a + b)¬≤ = a¬≤ + 2ab + b¬≤` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/|Έ¦|`) `ΡΛ_toy = (≈€_tot)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_A)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_¬§A)¬≤` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(≈€_tot)¬≤` (specialisation of `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared`). The standard fact used to project the toy ground state onto a fixed `(≈€_tot)¬≤` eigenspace, underpinning the `S_tot = 0` selection of the toy ground state | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `_complement` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(≈€_A)¬≤` and with `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤`. Direct consequences of the closed form `ΡΛ_toy = (≈€_tot)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_A)¬≤ βà£ (≈€_¬§A)¬≤` and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs Έ±-6o, Έ±-6s, self-commute trivially). Together with Έ±-6p, gives all four Casimir-style commutators of `ΡΛ_toy`, the prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis on which the `S_tot = 0` selection rests | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `ΡΛ_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|Έ¦\|¬Ζ(\|Έ¦\|+2)/4 βà£ \|A\|¬Ζ(\|A\|+2)/4 βà£ \|¬§A\|¬Ζ(\|¬§A\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|Έ¦\| = \|A\| + \|¬§A\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|¬Ζ\|¬§A\|/2`. The eigenvalue is non-negative for any bipartite lattice and strictly positive when both sublattices are non-empty | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **NΟ©el state** `ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(≈€_A)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü© = (\|A\|¬Ζ(\|A\|+2)/4) ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü©` and `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤ ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü© = (\|¬§A\|¬Ζ(\|¬§A\|+2)/4) ¬Ζ \|ΈΠ_NΟ©el(A)βü©`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the NΟ©el configuration is constant on each sublattice (`œÉ x = 0` on `A`, `œÉ x = 1` on `¬§A`); the NΟ©el state is simultaneously a `(≈€_A)¬≤` and `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (≈€tot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself βÄî operator-level form of Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `≈€tot¬≤` preserves every `H_M` (since `[≈€tot¬≤, ≈€tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : Έ¦ βÜ£ Έ¦ βÜ£ β³²` and `M : β³²`, `v βàà H_M` implies `H ¬Ζ v βàà H_M` βÄî the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, ≈€tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : β³²`, `v βàà H_M` implies `≈€tot^- ¬Ζ v βàà H_{M-1}` βÄî the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[≈€tot^(3), ≈€tot^-] = -≈€tot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : β³²`, `v βàà H_M` implies `≈€tot^+ ¬Ζ v βàà H_{M+1}` βÄî the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[≈€tot^(3), ≈€tot^+] = +≈€tot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `Έ¦ = Fin 2` and any `ΈΗ`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ) * onSite 1 (Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ)` (general-ΈΗ extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` βÄî bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ_tot = exp(-iΈΗ ≈€_tot^(Έ±))`. Composes `spinHalfRot{Έ±}_eq_exp` (single site), `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` (per-site bridge), and `Matrix.exp_sum_of_commute` (commuting-summand exp = noncommProd of exps) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.12) βÜ£ (2.2.13): `Commute A (≈€_tot^(Έ±)) βÜ£ Commute A (Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `Ο¦` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (≈€^¬±_tot) βÜ£ Commute A (exp(c βÄΔ ≈€^¬±_tot))` for any `c : β³²` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ_tot)α¥¥ * Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-iΈΗ ≈€_tot^(Έ±)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (≈€_tot^(Έ±)) βÜ£ (Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ_tot)α¥¥ * A * Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `IsInMagnetizationSubspace` | predicate for the magnetization-`M` eigenspace `H_M` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace M` | the magnetization-`M` eigenspace as a `Submodule β³² ((Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin 2) βÜ£ β³²)` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|œÉβü© βàà H_{|œÉ|/2}` βÄî basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M βä™ H_{M'} = βäΞ` (`M βâ† M'`) βÄî eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `iSup_magnetizationSubspace_eq_top` | `β®Ü_M H_M = βäΛ` βÄî every vector decomposes as a sum across sectors | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_eq_eigenspace` | bridge `H_M = (≈€_tot^(3) as End).eigenspace M` (used to inherit `iSupIndep`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_iSupIndep` | `iSupIndep`: each sector is disjoint from the supremum of all others | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_isInternal` | `DirectSum.IsInternal`: full direct-sum decomposition `H = βäï_M H_M` (Tasaki eqs. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfDot x y` | `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y := ΈΘ_{Έ±} onSite x ≈€^(Έ±) ¬Ζ onSite y ≈€^(Έ±)` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y = (1/2)(≈€_x^+ ≈€_y^- + ≈€_x^- ≈€_y^+) + ≈€_x^(3) ≈€_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_comm` | `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y = ≈€_y ¬Ζ ≈€_x` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_isHermitian` | `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` | `(≈€_tot)¬≤ = ΈΘ_{x,y} ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y, ≈€_tot^(Έ±)] = 0` for Έ± βàà {1, 2, 3} (SU(2) invariance, Tasaki (2.2.17)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y, ≈€^¬±_tot] = 0` (ladder-operator version of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y |βÜëβÜëβü© = (1/4) |βÜëβÜëβü©`, `≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y |βÜ™βÜ™βü© = (1/4) |βÜ™βÜ™βü©` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = ¬±1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, ≈€^¬±_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H ≈€tot¬≤` βÄî the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, ≈€tot^Έ±] = 0` for each Έ±, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `≈€tot¬≤ ¬Ζ v = S ¬Ζ v` then `≈€tot¬≤ ¬Ζ (H ¬Ζ v) = S ¬Ζ (H ¬Ζ v)` βÄî operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `œÉ^+ ¬Ζ œÉ^z = -œÉ^+` and `œÉ^z ¬Ζ œÉ^+ = œÉ^+` βÄî the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `≈€_tot¬≤ |s s βÄΠ sβü© = (N(N+2)/4) |s s βÄΠ sβü©` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : β³ï`, `≈€tot¬≤ ¬Ζ (≈€tot^βà™)^k ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü© = (|Έ¦|¬Ζ(|Έ¦|+2)/4) ¬Ζ (≈€tot^βà™)^k ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©` βÄî the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |Έ¦|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |sβÄΠsβü© = (ΈΘ_{x,y} J(x,y)¬Ζ(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©` βÄî every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-≈€_x¬Ζ≈€_y |ΈΠβÜëβü© = -S¬≤ |ΈΠβÜëβü©` for `S = 1/2`, `x βâ† y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) βÄî the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|¬ΖS¬≤`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H ¬Ζ (≈€_tot^+ ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©) = c_J ¬Ζ (≈€_tot^+ ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©)` βÄî `≈€_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `≈€_tot^-` βÄî the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|ΈΠ_Mβü©` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : β³ï`, `H ¬Ζ ((≈€_tot^-)^k ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©) = c_J ¬Ζ ((≈€_tot^-)^k ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 βÄî every iterate `(≈€_tot^-)^k ¬Ζ |ΈΠβÜëβü©` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|ΈΠβÜëβü©` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `≈€_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : β³ï`, `H ¬Ζ ((≈€_tot^+)^k ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©) = c_J ¬Ζ ((≈€_tot^+)^k ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `ΈΗ : β³ù`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ = exp(-iΈΗ ≈€_tot^Έ±)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Έ±}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{Έ±}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `ΈΗ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ¬Ζ (Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©) = c_J ¬Ζ (Ο¦^(Έ±)_ΈΗ ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©)` βÄî the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `ΈΗ`, `œï`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ¬Ζ (Ο¦^(3)_œï ¬Ζ Ο¦^(2)_ΈΗ ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©) = c_J ¬Ζ (Ο¦^(3)_œï ¬Ζ Ο¦^(2)_ΈΗ ¬Ζ |sβÄΠsβü©)` βÄî the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|Έû_ΈΗ,œïβü©` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `≈€_tot¬≤ (|βÜëβÜ™βü© - |βÜ™βÜëβü©) = 0` for `Έ¦ = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `≈€_tot¬≤ (|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©) = 2(|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©)` for `Έ¦ = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `≈€_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x SΈ±, ΈΘ_z onSite z SΈ≤] = onSite x [SΈ±, SΈ≤]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `œÉ = 2¬ΖS` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ΈΘ œÉ^z_i œÉ^z_{i+1} - h ΈΘ œÉ^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `œÉ^z_x ¬Ζ œÉ^z_y` (generic in `Έ¦`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : Έ¦ βÜ£ Έ¦ βÜ£ β³²) (h : β³²)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `ΈΘ J(x,y) œÉ^z_x œÉ^z_y βà£ h ΈΘ œÉ^x_x` for any finite `Έ¦` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G Έ≤ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `ΈΘ_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `ΈΘ_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i βÜΠ i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the NΟ©el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 βâΓ 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Testing infrastructure

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState Έ≤ J h N` | `gibbsState Έ≤ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `œ¹_Έ≤` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[œ¹_Έ≤, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `βü®Aβü©_0 = (1/dim) ¬Ζ Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(βü®Oβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `βü®[H_Ising, A]βü©_Έ≤ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(βü®H_Isingβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(βü®H_Ising ¬Ζ Oβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(βü®H_Ising^2βü©_Έ≤).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(βü®H_Ising^nβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` for any `n : β³ï` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(βü®A¬ΖB + B¬ΖAβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(βü®A¬ΖB βà£ B¬ΖAβü©_Έ≤).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_Έ≤(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn Έ≤ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((βü®Oβü©_Έ≤).re : β³²) = βü®Oβü©_Έ≤` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(œ¹_Έ≤^n) = Z(nΈ≤) / Z(Έ≤)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `βü®H_Isingβü©_Έ≤ = -J ¬Ζ βàë βü®œÉ^z œÉ^zβü©_Έ≤ + (-h) ¬Ζ βàë βü®œÉ^xβü©_Έ≤` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ¬ß3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp Έ≤ H` | `exp(-Έ≤H) := Matrix.exp (-Έ≤ βÄΔ H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-Έ≤H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0¬ΖH) = 1` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, pp. 75βÄ™78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(Έ≤β²¹+Έ≤β²²)H) = exp(-Έ≤β²¹H) ¬Ζ exp(-Έ≤β²²H)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-Έ≤(Hβ²¹+Hβ²²)) = exp(-Έ≤Hβ²¹) ¬Ζ exp(-Έ≤Hβ²²)` for commuting `Hβ²¹, Hβ²²` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(Έ≤H) ¬Ζ exp(-Έ≤H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-Έ≤H) ¬Ζ exp(Έ≤H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-Έ≤H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-Έ≤H) βâ† 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `œ¹_Έ≤ βâ† 0` when `Z(Έ≤) βâ† 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(œ¹_Έ≤)β¹Μ¬Ι = Z(Έ≤) ¬Ζ e^{Έ≤H}` when `Z(Έ≤) βâ† 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(Έ≤) ¬Ζ œ¹_Έ≤ = e^{-Έ≤H}` when `Z(Έ≤) βâ† 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(Έ≤) ¬Ζ βü®Aβü©_Έ≤ = Tr(e^{-Έ≤H} ¬Ζ A)` when `Z(Έ≤) βâ† 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n¬ΖΈ≤)H) = (exp(-Έ≤H))^n` for `n : β³ï` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2Έ≤)H) = exp(-Έ≤H) ¬Ζ exp(-Έ≤H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-Έ≤H))β¹Μ¬Ι = exp(Έ≤H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n¬ΖΈ≤)H) = (exp(-Έ≤H))^n` for `n : β³Λ` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn Έ≤ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-Έ≤H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (Έ¦ βÜ£ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) βâ† 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at Έ≤ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n β³²`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn Έ≤ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(œ¹_Έ≤¬≤) = Z(2Έ≤) / Z(Έ≤)¬≤` (purity / RΟ©nyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(œ¹_Έ≤^n) = Z(nΈ≤) / Z(Έ≤)^n` for any `n : β³ï` (RΟ©nyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `œ¹_0 = (1/dim) ¬Ζ I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `œ¹_0β¹Μ¬Ι = dim ¬Ζ I` (matrix inverse at Έ≤ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `βü®Aβü©_0 = (1/dim) ¬Ζ Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState Έ≤ H` | `œ¹ := (1/Z) βÄΔ exp(-Έ≤H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(œ¹) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `œ¹` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation Έ≤ H O` | `βü®Oβü© := Matrix.trace (œ¹ * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `βü®1βü© = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `βü®Oβ²¹ + Oβ²²βü© = βü®Oβ²¹βü© + βü®Oβ²²βü©` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `βü®c ¬Ζ Oβü© = c ¬Ζ βü®Oβü©` (scalar linearity, `c : β³²`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `βü®-Oβü© = -βü®Oβü©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `βü®A - Bβü© = βü®Aβü© - βü®Bβü©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `βü®βàë i βàà s, f iβü© = βàë i βàà s, βü®f iβü©` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-Έ≤H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[œ¹_Έ≤, H] = 0`, i.e. `œ¹_Έ≤` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ¬Ζ B)) = Tr(A ¬Ζ B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n β³²` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star βü®Oβü©_Έ≤ = βü®Oβü©_Έ≤` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(βü®Oβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((βü®Oβü©_Έ≤).re : β³²) = βü®Oβü©_Έ≤` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `βü®H ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤ = βü®A ¬Ζ Hβü©_Έ≤` for any `A` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `βü®A ¬Ζ Oβü©_Έ≤ = βü®O ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `βü®A ¬Ζ O βà£ O ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `βü®[H, A]βü©_Έ≤ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(βü®Hβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(βü®O ¬Ζ Oβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(βü®O^nβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : β³ï` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance Έ≤ H O` | `Var_Έ≤(O) := βü®O ¬Ζ Oβü©_Έ≤ βà£ βü®Oβü©_Έ≤¬≤` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_Έ≤(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at Έ≤ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ¬Ζ Tr(O¬≤) βà£ ((1/dim) ¬Ζ Tr O)¬≤` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_Έ≤(O) = βü®(O βà£ βü®Oβü©_Έ≤ ¬Ζ 1) ¬Ζ (O βà£ βü®Oβü©_Έ≤ ¬Ζ 1)βü©_Έ≤` (centered-square form, `Z βâ† 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance Έ≤ H A B` | `Cov_Έ≤(A, B) := βü®A ¬Ζ Bβü©_Έ≤ βà£ βü®Aβü©_Έ≤ ¬Ζ βü®Bβü©_Έ≤` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_Έ≤(O, O) = Var_Έ≤(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_Έ≤(A, B) βà£ Cov_Έ≤(B, A) = βü®A ¬Ζ B βà£ B ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_Έ≤(Aβ²¹ + Aβ²², B) = Cov_Έ≤(Aβ²¹, B) + Cov_Έ≤(Aβ²², B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_Έ≤(A, Bβ²¹ + Bβ²²) = Cov_Έ≤(A, Bβ²¹) + Cov_Έ≤(A, Bβ²²)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_Έ≤(c βÄΔ A, B) = c ¬Ζ Cov_Έ≤(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_Έ≤(A, c βÄΔ B) = c ¬Ζ Cov_Έ≤(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_Έ≤(c βÄΔ 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_Έ≤(A, c βÄΔ 1) = 0` (when `Z βâ† 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_Έ≤(c βÄΔ 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_Έ≤(A, c βÄΔ 1) = 0` (when `Z βâ† 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm Έ≤ H A B` | `Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B) := (1/2) ¬Ζ βü®A ¬Ζ B + B ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤ βà£ βü®Aβü©_Έ≤ ¬Ζ βü®Bβü©_Έ≤` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_Έ≤(O, O) = Var_Έ≤(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B) = Cov^s_Έ≤(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_Έ≤` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_Έ≤` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_Έ≤(A + B) = Var_Έ≤(A) + Var_Έ≤(B) + 2 ¬Ζ Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_Έ≤(1) = 0` (when `Z βâ† 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_Έ≤(c βÄΔ A) = c¬≤ ¬Ζ Var_Έ≤(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_Έ≤(c βÄΔ 1) = 0` (when `Z βâ† 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_Έ≤(βà£A) = Var_Έ≤(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_Έ≤(A + c βÄΔ 1) = Var_Έ≤(A)` (when `Z βâ† 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_Έ≤(A, B) = Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B) + (1/2) ¬Ζ βü®A ¬Ζ B βà£ B ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B) = (1/2) ¬Ζ (Cov_Έ≤(A, B) + Cov_Έ≤(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_Έ≤(A, B) = Cov^s_Έ≤(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(œ¹ ¬Ζ X) = Tr(œ¹ ¬Ζ Xα¥¥)` for Hermitian `œ¹` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star βü®A ¬Ζ Bβü©_Έ≤ = βü®B ¬Ζ Aβü©_Έ≤` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | `(βü®A¬ΖB + B¬ΖAβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` (anticommutator is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | `(βü®A¬ΖB βà£ B¬ΖAβü©_Έ≤).re = 0` (commutator is purely imaginary) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | `(βü®H ¬Ζ Oβü©_Έ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ¬ß3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `Έ¦ βÜ£ Έ¦ βÜ£ β³²` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val βà® ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) βÜ£ Fin (N+1) βÜ£ β³²`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) βÜ£ Fin (N+2) βÜ£ β³²`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ΈΘ_{x,y} J(x,y) ≈€_x ¬Ζ ≈€_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : β³²`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) Έ≤ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: Έ≤ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `βü®[H, A]βü© = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((βü®Oβü©_Έ≤).re : β³²) = βü®Oβü©_Έ≤`, RΟ©nyi-n trace `Tr(œ¹_Έ≤^n) = Z(nΈ≤) / Z(Έ≤)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph N Έ≤ G J U` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Hubbard Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardChainGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridge: `hubbardChainGibbsState = hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J) U` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
