| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ¬Ζ spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ¬ß2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ¬Ζ |βÜëβÜëβü© = -(J/2) ¬Ζ |βÜëβÜëβü©` βÄî `|βÜëβÜëβü©` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ¬Ζ (|βÜëβÜ™βü© - |βÜ™βÜëβü©) = (3J/2) ¬Ζ (|βÜëβÜ™βü© - |βÜ™βÜëβü©)` βÄî singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ¬Ζ |βÜ™βÜ™βü© = -(J/2) ¬Ζ |βÜ™βÜ™βü©` βÄî all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ¬Ζ (|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©) = -(J/2) ¬Ζ (|βÜëβÜ™βü© + |βÜ™βÜëβü©)` βÄî triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ¬Ζ (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` βÄî explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ¬Ζ |βÜëβÜëβÜëβü© = -J ¬Ζ |βÜëβÜëβÜëβü©` βÄî confirming the linear scaling `E(|βÜë..βÜëβü©) = -N¬ΖJ/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : β³ï`, `ΈΘ_{x,y βàà Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ¬Ζ J : β³²)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : β³ï`, `J : β³ù`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ¬Ζ |s..sβü© = -(N¬ΖJ/2 : β³²) ¬Ζ |s..sβü©` βÄî both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|¬ΖS¬≤` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N¬ΖJ/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : β³ï`, `J : β³ù`, `k : β³ï`, `H_open ¬Ζ ((≈€tot^-)^k ¬Ζ |βÜë..βÜëβü©) = -(N¬ΖJ/2 : β³²) ¬Ζ ((≈€tot^-)^k ¬Ζ |βÜë..βÜëβü©)` βÄî the unnormalised Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|ΈΠ_Mβü©` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|βÜ™..βÜ™βü©`, same eigenvalue `-(N¬ΖJ/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AβÄ™C).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal βÜ£ primitive (Seneta ¬ß1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) βâΛ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)¬Ζx βâΛ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ¬ß1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r¬Ζv`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` βüΙ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ΈΦv`, `v βâΞ 0`, `v βâ† 0` βüΙ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg βüΙ βàÉ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, pp. 27βÄ™28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg βüΙ βàÉ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, pp. 27βÄ™28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_nonneg_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry**, retained for docs) symmetric nonneg max eigenvalue has nonneg eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian βüΙ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `ΈΦ` βüΙ every real eigenvector at `ΈΦ` is `s βÄΔ v` for some scalar `s` (the `ΈΦ`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t βÄΔ u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `ΈΦ`, hence `βàù v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u βàù v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ¬ß1.2 (pp. 27βÄ™28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallβÄ™LiebβÄ™Mattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> βÄî the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(≈€β¹Μ_tot)^M|all-upβü©` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0βÜ£1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronβÄ™Frobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[ΡΛ, ≈€β¹Μ_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { œÉ : V βÜ£ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS œÉ = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ΈΠ` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `ΈΦ` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `Έ≥` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card¬ΖN/2βà£M)(card¬ΖN/2βà£Mβà£1)`, then `≈€β¹Μ_tot ¬Ζ magSectorEmbedding ΈΠ` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `ΈΦ` (via `[ΡΛ, ≈€β¹Μ_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronβÄ™Frobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `≈€β¹Κ_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card¬ΖN/2βà£K)(card¬ΖN/2βà£K+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `≈€_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A œÉ = βàë_{xβààA}(N/2βà£œÉ_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(≈€_A)¬≤`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(≈€_A)¬≤`/`(≈€_¬§A)¬≤` eigenvalues `Έ≥_A, Έ≥_B` obey `Έ≥_A.re βâΛ s_A(s_A+1)`, `Έ≥_B.re βâΛ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToySublatticeBounds.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|¬§A|)¬ΖN`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|¬ΖN/2 βà£ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A βà£ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|¬§A|)¬ΖN`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `ΈΦ βâΛ predicted βà£ s_A(s_A+1) βà£ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(≈€_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness βÄî exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `hΈΦ` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `ΈΦ`) has `ΈΦ βâΛ E` for every sector eigenvector `œÜ βâ† 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `hΈΦ` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted βà£ s_A(s_A+1) βà£ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_Aβà£s_B|`, a non-zero `(≈€_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector at `Έ≥_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `Έ≥_A βâΛ s_A(s_A+1)`, `Έ≥_B βâΛ s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(Έ≥_totβà£Έ≥_Aβà£Έ≥_B).re βâΛ predicted βà£ s_A(s_A+1) βà£ s_B(s_B+1)` has `Έ≥_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschβÄ™Gordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(≈€_tot)¬≤ βâΞ m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(≈€_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector at `Έ≥` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 βâΛ M.re` has `M.re¬Ζ(M.re+1) βâΛ Έ≥.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `≈€β¹Κ_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' βâΞ M`, giving `Έ≥ = M'(M'+1) βâΞ M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_Aβà£s_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschβÄ™Gordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(≈€_tot)¬≤`, `(≈€_A)¬≤`, `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤` at `(Έ≥_tot, Έ≥_A, Έ≥_B)` is a `ΡΛ_toy`-eigenvector at `Έ≥_tot βà£ Έ≥_A βà£ Έ≥_B` (from `ΡΛ_toy = (≈€_tot)¬≤βà£(≈€_A)¬≤βà£(≈€_¬§A)¬≤`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(≈€_A)¬≤ βâΛ s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `Έ≥` of `(≈€_A)¬≤` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `Έ≥.re βâΛ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|¬ΖN/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(≈€_A)¬≤`-eigenvector at `Έ≥` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_Aβà£k)`, repeatedly raising with `≈€_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `βäΞ`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(≈€_A)¬≤`-eigenvector at the same `Έ≥`. Sublattice analogue of `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (Issue #3658, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeightExistence.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(≈€_A)¬≤` commutes with `≈€_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(≈€_A)¬≤`-eigenvector at `Έ≥` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_Aβà£k) v` that is again a `(≈€_A)¬≤`-eigenvector at `Έ≥` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (Issue #3658, PR 10). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagWeightComponent.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagProjFn` / `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq` (with `sublatticeMagSumS`, `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_eq_sub`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice magnetization projection & decomposition**: the pointwise projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M v` (keeps `v œÉ` where `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A œÉ = M`) lands in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M`, is linear, and the projectors over the finite spectrum `s_A βà£ k` (`k=0..|A|¬ΖN`) sum to the identity (`sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A œÉ = s_A βà£ sublatticeMagSumS A œÉ`, `sublatticeMagSumS A œÉ = βàë_{xβààA}œÉ_x βâΛ |A|¬ΖN`). Sublattice analogue of `magProjFn`/`sum_magProjFn_eq` (Issue #3658, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagProjection.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_im_zero`) | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir bound**: a non-zero highest-weight `(≈€_A)¬≤`-eigenvector (`≈€_A^+ w = 0`) at `Έ≥` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` satisfies `Έ≥.re βâΛ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|¬ΖN/2`. Its Casimir value is `M(M+1)` (#3663), `M` is an attained basis eigenvalue (#3667 βäΞ-lemma), so `|M| βâΛ s_A` (#3660). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (Issue #3658, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirHighestWeightBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_diag` / `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_off_diag` / `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum` | **Sublattice magnetization spectrum**: `≈€_A^(3)` is diagonal with entry `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A œÉ` on the diagonal and zero off it; hence a value `M` outside the diagonal spectrum gives `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M = βäΞ`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOp3_apply_diag/off_diag` and `magSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum`; lets the highest-weight argument conclude an attained sublattice magnetization is an actual basis eigenvalue (so `|M| βâΛ s_A`) (Issue #3658, PR 7). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagSpectrum.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` | **Sublattice ladder shifts the sublattice grading**: `≈€_A^+` maps `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M βÜ£ A (M+1)` and `≈€_A^-` maps it to `A (Mβà£1)`, from the grading commutators `[≈€_A^(3), ≈€_A^¬±] = ¬±≈€_A^¬±`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`; drives the termination of the highest-weight raising for `(≈€_A)¬≤` (Issue #3658, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagShift.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir relation**: if `≈€_A^(3) w = m_A¬Ζw` and `≈€_A^+ w = 0`, then `(≈€_A)¬≤ w = m_A(m_A+1)¬Ζw`. From the sublattice Cartan identity `≈€_A^- ≈€_A^+ = (≈€_A^1)¬≤+(≈€_A^2)¬≤βà£≈€_A^(3)` (so `(≈€_A)¬≤ = ≈€_A^-≈€_A^+ + ≈€_A^(3) + (≈€_A^(3))¬≤`); on a highest-weight `≈€_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `≈€_A^-≈€_A^+` term vanishes. Sublattice analogue of the total highest-weight relation; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 4) for the sublattice Casimir spectral max bound. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice lowest-weight Casimir relation**: if `≈€_A^(3) w = m_A¬Ζw` and `≈€_A^- w = 0`, then `(≈€_A)¬≤ w = m_A(m_Aβà£1)¬Ζw`. From the other sublattice Cartan identity `≈€_A^+ ≈€_A^- = (≈€_A^1)¬≤+(≈€_A^2)¬≤+≈€_A^(3)` (so `(≈€_A)¬≤ = ≈€_A^+≈€_A^- βà£ ≈€_A^(3) + (≈€_A^(3))¬≤`); on a lowest-weight vector the `≈€_A^+≈€_A^-` term vanishes. At `m_A = βà£s_A` this gives `(≈€_A)¬≤ = s_A(s_A+1)` (max), so the all-down sublattice state realizes the maximal `(≈€_A)¬≤` βÄî a building block of the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLowestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_antiAligned_eq_max` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_complement_antiAligned_eq_max` (with `antiAlignedConfigS`) | **Anti-aligned state realizes the maximal sublattice Casimirs**: the basis config `antiAlignedConfigS A N` (all `A`-sites up `œÉ=0`, all `¬§A`-sites down `œÉ=N`) satisfies `(≈€_A)¬≤ |œÉβ²Äβü© = s_A(s_A+1) |œÉβ²Äβü©` and `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤ |œÉβ²Äβü© = s_B(s_B+1) |œÉβ²Äβü©` βÄî the `A`-block is the sublattice highest weight (#3663), the `¬§A`-block the sublattice lowest weight (#3681). Total magnetization `s_A βà£ s_B` (the extremal sector); projecting onto `(≈€_tot)¬≤=predicted` yields the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23AntialignedJointEigenvector.lean`) |

Inspection of the ladder link (`tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ`/`_pred`) shows only the total-Casimir eigenvalue (vs. the lowering/raising-kernel value) is consumed; the sublattice components are never used. This matters for the sound route because the ground state of an arbitrary bipartite `J` carries the predicted *total* Casimir (overlap pin) but need not be a sublattice-Casimir eigenvector, so it is not in the joint subspace. Since `≈€β¹Μ_tot` commutes with all three Casimir operators (`totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`) the eigenvalues are preserved, and lowering is non-vanishing because the predicted value differs from the sector's lowering-kernel value. The base sector `M = min(|A|,|¬§A|)¬ΖN` re-derives #3709 in embedded form; the general sector is reached by induction on the distance from the base, lowering with `tasaki23_jointPredicted_lowering_succ` (#3714) and re-embedding via `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`. This is the per-sector predicted-Casimir input the constancy chain (#3713) and overlap pin (#3712) consume, obtained without any lowered Marshall-positivity ("site-sum") hypothesis (Issue #3542). | `tasaki23_toy_min_energy_arith` | **Arithmetic core of the toy minimum-energy bound** (option (a) toward interior-sector predicted Casimir): for real spin values `0 βâΛ a βâΛ s_A`, `0 βâΛ b βâΛ s_B βâΛ s_A`, `f(a,b) := |aβà£b|(|aβà£b|+1) βà£ a(a+1) βà£ b(b+1)` is minimised at the corner `(s_A, s_B)`, i.e. `f(s_A, s_B) βâΛ f(a, b)`. Combined with the coupled total-spin lower bound `(≈€_tot)¬≤ βâΞ |aβà£b|(|aβà£b|+1)` on the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace `W_{a,b}` (the ClebschβÄ™Gordan triangle inequality, in progress), this gives `ΡΛ_toy βâΞ bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` on each `W_{a,b}`, hence the global toy minimum-energy bound; see `.self-local/tex/3716-tasaki-2-5-toy-min-energy-bound.tex` (Issue #3542). From the Cartan identities `(≈€_A)¬≤ = ≈€_A^βà™ ≈€_A^¬± + ≈€_A^(3)(≈€_A^(3)¬±1)` and the positive semidefiniteness `βü®v, ≈€_A^βà™ ≈€_A^¬± vβü© = βÄ•≈€_A^¬± vβÄ•¬≤ βâΞ 0` (using `(≈€_A^¬±)^βÄ† = ≈€_A^βà™`). This is the magnitude bound (`|q| βâΛ b` on the `(≈€_A)¬≤=b(b+1)` eigenspace) that excludes spurious sublattice weights at the coupled total-spin lower bound's recurrence boundary; see `.self-local/tex/3717-coupled-total-spin-lower-bound.tex` (Issue #3542). | `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero_sublatticeSpinSOp3_re_nonpos` | **A sublattice lowest weight has non-positive magnetization** (option (a), Route 5 brick 1): if `v βâ† 0`, `≈€_A^(3) v = q v` and `≈€_A^- v = 0`, then `q.re βâΛ 0`. The lowest-weight Casimir relation `(≈€_A)¬≤ = q(qβà£1)` plus the magnitude bound `(≈€_A)¬≤ βâΞ q(q+1)` (brick 0) force `q(qβà£1) βâΞ q(q+1)`, i.e. `q.re βâΛ 0`. | `sublatticeMagProjFn_of_mem`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_of_mem_ne`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mulVec_of_commute`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_sublatticeSpinSquaredS`(`_complement`) | **Sublattice magnetization-projection infrastructure** (option (a), Route 5 brick 2a): the pointwise sublattice-`A` magnetization projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M` is idempotent on its own subspace, annihilates other subspaces, and commutes with any operator commuting with `≈€_A^(3)` (proved via the magnetization-level decomposition `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`). Hence the magnetization component of an `(≈€_A)¬≤`- (resp. `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤`-) eigenvector is again a Casimir eigenvector at the same eigenvalue βÄî the Casimir-inheritance needed for the bottom-component extraction of the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). | `exists_sublattice_A_lowestWeight_component` | **Bottom sublattice-`A` magnetization component is `≈€_A^-`-killed** (option (a), Route 5 brick 2b): a total lowest-weight `w` (`≈€β¹Μ_tot w = 0`, total magnetization `m`, simultaneous sublattice-Casimir eigenvector at `Έ±, Έ≤`) yields a non-zero `≈€_A^-`-killed simultaneous weight vector `v` with `≈€_A^(3) v = p v`, `≈€_¬§A^(3) v = (mβà£p) v` and the inherited Casimir eigenvalues `Έ±, Έ≤`. Obtained as the minimal-`A`-magnetization nonzero component (max over the magnetization-level projections): projecting `≈€β¹Μ_tot w = ≈€_A^- w + ≈€_¬§A^- w = 0` onto the level just below `p` leaves only `≈€_A^- v` (the `≈€_¬§A^-` part preserves `A`-magnetization and sees only the vanishing lower component). | `totalLowestWeight_re_le_complement_sub` | **One-sided total-magnetization bound** (option (a), Route 5 brick 3a): a total lowest weight `w` in the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace with spins `a, b` (`(≈€_A)¬≤ = a(a+1)`, `(≈€_¬§A)¬≤ = b(b+1)`) has `m.re βâΛ b βà£ a`. Combined with its `A βÜî ¬§A` mirror this yields `|m| βâΞ |a βà£ b|` βÄî the ClebschβÄ™Gordan triangle inequality (Issue #3542). | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for the bipartite toy coupling** (#3887 extension chain, Thm23-#3887.12βÄ™.21, PR #3891): the canonical `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` API is now the structural statement, dropping the vacuous-at-`N=1` `h_intermediate` hypothesis (`βàÄ œ³, βàÄ x, βàÉ z, A z βâ† A x βàß (œ³ z).val < N`; unsatisfiable when `œ³ βâΓ Fin.last 1`). The toy capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` proves the conclusion from `(1 βâΛ N, 1 βâΛ |A|, 1 βâΛ |¬§A|)`, the genuine physical hypotheses, by chaining structural variants of the entire MLM / sector existence / Casimir / lower-bound stack: complex sector Marshall eigenvector (#3887.12), full-Hilbert lift (#3887.13), four-level Marshall-positive uniqueness chain (#3887.14), Theorem 2.2 bundled full (#3887.15), Theorem 2.3 sector existence wrapper (#3887.16), toy sector energy lower bound (#3887.17), per-sector toy ground state at predicted (#3887.19), final capstone (#3887.21). | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for general bipartite J** (PR #3893, Issue #3892): the canonical general-`J` closure proves `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3 A N J c` for ANY real symmetric non-negative bipartite coupling `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph at any `N βâΞ 1`, under canonical orientation `|¬§A| βâΛ |A|` with `s_B > 0`. This removes BOTH the toy-coupling restriction of PR #3891 and the vacuous-at-N=1 `h_intermediate` restriction of the older #3738 capstone. At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure for general bipartite J**. Chain: structural sector lift + Casimir (Step 1), structural adjacent-sector common-energy step (Step 2), structural common ground-state energy (Step 3, TIER 4 constancy), capstone (Step 4) combining Step 3 with PR #3891's `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence` and the already-`h_intermediate`-free `tasaki23_general_hOutside` + `tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` + `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebβÄ™Mattis, J. Math. Phys. 3 (1962) 749 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralGeneralFinal.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 partial conclusion: unique GS has zero `≈€¬≥_tot` magnetization** (Issue #3739, PR #3896): if the anisotropic Heisenberg ground-state eigenspace has `finrank β³² βâΛ 1`, every non-zero ground state `ΈΠ` satisfies `≈€¬≥_tot ΈΠ = 0`. Proof: ΈΠ, ≈€¬≥_tot ΈΠ, Έ‰ ΈΠ all lie in the GS eigenspace (`H` commutes with `≈€¬≥_tot` (#3741) and with the reflection `Έ‰` (#3745)). From `finrank βâΛ 1` + ΈΠ βâ† 0 and `Module.finrank_le_one_iff`, `≈€¬≥_tot ΈΠ = Έ≥ ΈΠ` and `Έ‰ ΈΠ = Έ¥ ΈΠ` for some scalars. `Έ‰¬≤ = 1` βüΙ `Έ¥¬≤ = 1` βüΙ `Έ¥ βâ† 0`. Apply the anticommutation `Έ‰ ≈€¬≥_tot = -≈€¬≥_tot Έ‰` (#3744) to ΈΠ: get `Έ¥Έ≥ βÄΔ ΈΠ = -Έ≥Έ¥ βÄΔ ΈΠ` βüΙ `2Έ¥Έ≥ βÄΔ ΈΠ = 0` βüΙ `Έ¥Έ≥ = 0` βüΙ `Έ≥ = 0` (since Έ¥ βâ† 0). This is the second conclusion of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 once uniqueness is established (uniqueness itself awaits the deformation argument from the SU(2) point, separate forthcoming work). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24ZeroMagnetizationFromUniqueness.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_at_SU2_eigenspace_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS` / `heisenbergHamiltonianS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 SU(2) base case via Theorem 2.3 reduction** (Issue #3739, PR #3897): at the SU(2) point `(ΈΜ=1, D=0)`, the anisotropic Hamiltonian reduces to the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian (via `anisotropicHeisenbergS_one_zero`), so every eigenspace coincides. The first lemma gives the direct eigenspace identification; the second specializes PR #3896 to the SU(2) point, giving `≈€¬≥_tot|ΈΠβü©=0` for any non-zero GS of the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian under the GS uniqueness hypothesis. Prepares the SU(2) base case for the deformation argument: once uniqueness is established at the SU(2) point (for symmetric `|A|=|¬§A|` via Theorem 2.2 + SU(2) singlet structure, separate forthcoming work), the `≈€¬≥_tot|ΈΠβü©=0` conclusion follows immediately. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24SU2BaseCase.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_apply_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 sector-crossing matrix entries vanish** (Issue #3739, PR #3898): the matrix entry `anisotropicHeisenbergS J ΈΜ D N œÉ œ³ = 0` whenever `magSumS œÉ βâ† magSumS œ³` βÄî the matrix-element form of U(1) invariance `[H, ≈€¬≥_tot] = 0` (#3741). Proof: evaluate `H ¬Ζ ≈€¬≥_tot = ≈€¬≥_tot ¬Ζ H` at `(œÉ, œ³)`; since `≈€¬≥_tot` is diagonal with entries `magEigenvalueS`, get `H œÉ œ³ ¬Ζ M_œ³ = M_œÉ ¬Ζ H œÉ œ³`. When `magSumS œÉ βâ† magSumS œ³`, Nat-cast injectivity gives `magEigenvalueS œÉ βâ† magEigenvalueS œ³`, so `H œÉ œ³ = 0`. Key building block for sector-decomposition arguments toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a) uniqueness. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_magSectorProjection_eigen` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 sector projection of eigenvector is eigenvector** (Issue #3739, PR #3899): for an eigenvector `v` of the anisotropic Hamiltonian at `ΈΦ`, the sector projection `magSectorEmbedding (magSectorRestriction (M:=M) v)` is also an eigenvector at the same `ΈΦ`. With `D βâΞ 0` and the same-site Marshall sign `+1` (the `¬±2` shift is even) this is the non-positive dressed single-ion off-diagonal contribution. For finite-dim `T, P` with `Tβà‰P = Pβà‰T` and `Pβà‰P = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `ΈΦ` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `¬±1`. The `βâΛ` was tight because the two intersected `¬±1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Combines (i.1) #3846 with the existing `isHermitian_eigenvalue_star_eq`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43βÄ™44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SubmatrixEigenvalueReal.lean`) |
| `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (with `hermitian_min_eigenvalue_mem_image`, `hermitian_min_eigenvalue_le`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_min_eigenvalue`, `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_min_eigenvalue`) | **(i.3) submatrix minimum eigenvalue (Hermitian)** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3848): for a Hermitian matrix `M` on a non-empty finite-dim space, the minimum of its eigenvalue function (`Matrix.IsHermitian.eigenvalues`) is well-defined as a real number via `Finset.min'`. The min is in the image of the eigenvalue function (hence one of the eigenvalues) and βâΛ every eigenvalue. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43βÄ™44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SubmatrixMinEigenvalue.lean`) |
| `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` | **(i.4) Hermitian eigenspace = βäΞ below min eigenvalue** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3849): for a Hermitian matrix `M` on a finite-dim complex space, if `ΈΦ < hermitianMinEigenvalue M`, then the eigenspace at `(ΈΦ : β³²)` is `βäΞ`. Proof via spectrum-eigenvalues bridge (`HasEigenvalue.mem_spectrum` βÜ£ `Matrix.spectrum_toLin'` βÜ£ `spectrum.algebraMap_mem_iff` βÜ£ `IsHermitian.spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`). Rules out eigenvalues below the minimum. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43βÄ™44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianEigenspaceBotBelowMin.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_both_block_mins` (with `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_both_block_mins`) | **(i.5) block-diag eigenspace = βäΞ below joint per-block minimum** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3850): for a parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`, if `ΈΦ : β³ù` is strictly less than the minimum eigenvalues of BOTH per-block submatrices, then the full eigenspace at `(ΈΦ : β³²)` is `βäΞ`. Combines (h.3) #3842 + (i.4) #3849. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43βÄ™44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockEigBotBelowJointMin.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_at_min_block_mins` | **(i.6) full eig βâΛ 2 at min(per-block mins) given per-block βâΛ 1 at min** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3851): the FINAL assembly step. Given per-block bounds `finrank βâΛ 1` at the per-block minimum eigenvalues, the full-eigenspace at `min(min_0, min_1)` has `finrank βâΛ 2`. Case analysis: `min_0 = min_1` both contribute `βâΛ 1`; `min_0 βâ† min_1` the higher block contributes `0` by (i.4) #3849, the lower `βâΛ 1`. Bridge from per-block PF simplicity to the unconditional global ground-state degeneracy `βâΛ 2` (modulo identification of PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43βÄ™44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockMinLeTwo.lean`) |

Discharges the block-diag (via `axisSwapped_apply_eq_zero_of_magSum_parity_ne`) and commute (via `axisSwapped_commute_magParityDiagS`) hypotheses inline. General `N` version requires an `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. **Analogue of (g.5) #3837 with per-block-min hypotheses threading the Hermitian spectral chain βÄî the remaining gap is identification of the per-sector PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue submatrix_p` (which would discharge h0/h1 to yield the unconditional ground-state degeneracy βâΛ 2 for bare anisotropic `ΡΛ`).** Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43βÄ™44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicMinLeTwo.lean`) |

For each parity block, if a PF eigenvalue `ΈΫ` has `finrank <= 1` and equals that block's Hermitian minimum, then the block has `finrank <= 1` at the full anisotropic ground energy: if the full ground energy equals the block minimum, this is the conditional theorem; if it is lower, `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` makes the block eigenspace zero. The path wrapper supplies both parity blocks along `Έ≥(t)`, and the target wrappers feed the result to the block-path bridge above. The raw-support abbreviations name the strict, `lambda = 1`, and `D = 0` reachability/PF inputs for one parity block. The one-block selector chooses among the fixed raw-support consumers by the case-(ii) path inequalities and leaves only the corner `(lambda,D)=(1,0)` PF/min callback explicit; the even/odd raw-support wrapper applies this selector to both parity blocks, and the full-min wrapper feeds those callbacks into the block-path PF/min transfer. The raw-support target wrappers derive the support-zero hypothesis from `hJself` and `hJbip`, then expose target uniqueness and zero magnetization directly from the raw-support input surface. The reachability wrappers use finite parity blocks to supply strict diagonal shifts automatically, so the public reachability-level target wrappers require only the even and odd strict, ion-only, and bond-only reachability totality hypotheses plus the two explicit corner callbacks. Hence the non-corner path never enters the `ΈΜ = 1`, `D < 0` ion-only branch, whose totality requires `2 <= N`; strict parity reachability and the `D = 0` bond-only branch both need only `1 <= N`. | `caseIIParityGaugeSign` / `caseIIParityGaugeSign_mul_eq_one_of_magSumS_eq` / `caseIIParityGaugeSign_mul_eq_neg_one_of_magSumS_add_two` / `caseIIParityGaugeSign_mul_eq_neg_one_of_add_two_magSumS` / `caseIIParityGaugeDiagonalOnParity_mul_self` | **General spin-S case-(ii) parity-block gauge sign layer** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): introduces the additional parity-block gauge `(-1)^(magSumS / 2)` needed for the `ΈΜ >= 1`, `D <= 0` PF route. On a fixed parity block it agrees with Tasaki's `(-1)^((magSumS - p) / 2)` convention up to a constant block sign. The gauge product is `1` when `magSumS` is preserved and `-1` when one configuration has `magSumS` two larger than the other, so it preserves transverse raise-lower signs while flipping the parity-bond and single-ion `¬±2` signs. The diagonal gauge squares to the identity and is ready to combine with the Marshall diagonal in the next shifted PF matrix layer. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIParityGauge.lean`) |

These are the SU(2) obstruction statements for a vanished adjacent-sector ladder move, proved from the total-spin Casimir rearrangements `≈€^+≈€^- = ≈€¬≤ - (≈€^z)¬≤ + ≈€^z` and `≈€^-≈€^+ = ≈€¬≤ - (≈€^z)¬≤ - ≈€^z`. | `tasaki23_totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_value` / `tasaki23_totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_value` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 Casimir-based ladder non-vanishing**: if a nonzero vector in the `≈€_tot^(3)` eigenspace of eigenvalue `m` has total-Casimir eigenvalue `Έ≥`, then `Έ≥ βâ† m * (m - 1)` forces `≈€^-_tot Έ® βâ† 0`, and `Έ≥ βâ† m * (m + 1)` forces `≈€^+_tot Έ® βâ† 0`. These are the contrapositive forms of the ladder-kernel Casimir consequences and are the bridge from the SU(2) obstruction to the sector interval argument: away from the representation endpoint value, the adjacent-sector ladder move cannot vanish. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Casimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_value` / `tasaki23_totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_value` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 sector-embedded Casimir ladder non-vanishing**: the Casimir-based non-vanishing criteria are specialized to zero-extended `magSumS = M` sector vectors. This packages the abstract `magSubspaceS` obstruction with `magSectorEmbedding_mem_magSubspaceS`, making it directly usable in the adjacent-sector chain. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Casimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_marshallPositive_magSectorEmbedding_ne_zero` / `tasaki23_totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_marshallPositive_magSectorEmbedding_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_value` / `tasaki23_totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_marshallPositive_magSectorEmbedding_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_value` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 Marshall-positive sector Casimir ladder non-vanishing**: a sector vector with strictly positive Marshall coefficients has nonzero zero-extension, because its value at any sector configuration is a nonzero real multiple of the Marshall sign. The lowered component is also expanded as a sum of the single-site lowering contributions `βàë x, ≈€^-_x Έ®_M`; therefore the same package can be invoked from the local site-sum strict positivity hypothesis. This combines non-vanishing from strict Marshall positivity, ladder eigenvalue preservation, the sector-support shift, and the #869 target-sector uniqueness theorem; the remaining critical-path input is to prove the sitewise Marshall-positivity hypothesis for the lowered vector. Under source-sector Marshall positivity, the signed real contribution is strictly positive for `A x = false` and strictly negative for `A x = true`, matching the single-site Marshall predecessor sign change. | `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_nonneg_of_A_false` / `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_nonpos_of_A_true` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_nonneg` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_onA_sum_nonpos` / `tasaki23_exists_lowerable_offA_of_offA_occupation_pos` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_pos_of_exists_lowerable_offA` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_pos_of_offA_occupation_pos` | **Boundary-inclusive lowered local sign bounds and sublattice filtered sums for Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the strict local lowering split is extended across the zero-target boundary. Off `A`, every signed single-site lowering contribution is non-negative; on `A`, every signed single-site lowering contribution is non-positive. Summing over the filtered finite sets gives a non-negative off-`A` signed sum and a non-positive on-`A` signed sum. Positive off-`A` occupation produces an actually lowerable off-`A` witness, and either that witness or the aggregate occupation hypothesis gives strict positivity of the off-`A` filtered sum by `Finset.sum_pos'`. These are decomposition bounds for the remaining strict lowered site-sum positivity proof, not yet the dominance argument for the full sum. The explicit lowerable coefficient can therefore be read as the matching predecessor raising coefficient times the positive predecessor source value, aligning the attached coefficient sums with the real source-weight raising sums. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSource.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_lowerable_positive_source_attach_sum_eq_raising_predecessor_source_sum` / `tasaki23_lowerable_positive_source_attach_sum_lt_of_raising_predecessor_source_sum_lt` / `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_sum_lt_of_lowerable_positive_source_attach_sum_lt` | **Lowerable predecessor raising-source sums for Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the pointwise lowerable coefficient mirror is lifted to attached finite sums over lowerable sites, rewriting explicit lowerable positive-source coefficient sums as sums of predecessor raising coefficients times positive predecessor source values. The dominance wrappers convert strict dominance in either direction between the explicit lowerable coefficient sums and the matching predecessor raising-source sums, so the remaining local comparison can be stated in the coefficient form or the raising-source form as needed by the final and common-energy boundaries. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSource.lean`) |

Consequently the attached predecessor raising-source sums used by the final dominance callback are non-negative. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSourceSum.lean`) |

The attached sum over lowerable sites is rewritten as the corresponding ambient finite-set sum, and the total lowerable predecessor raising-source sum is split into the on-`A` and off-`A` attached sums. This supplies the finite-set partition needed before applying the real predecessor source-weight equation to the two sublattice raising-source sums. This states the local comparison in the difference form naturally produced by the real predecessor source-weight identity. The positivity bridges rewrite the lowerable attached-sum difference from the real source-weight callback to this boundary form and therefore prove the lowered-vector Marshall-positive component; the source-form site-sum bridge also turns the same difference directly into the strict single-site lowered sum callback. The named callback adapter exposes this site-sum form as the API consumed by the new interval chain and final theorem wrapper: predicted-GS membership supplies the re-embedded source-weight identity and lowered sublattice-Casimir/support data at each successor step, the predecessor-difference callback is converted to strict lowered site-sum positivity, and the direct site-sum successor chain finishes the common-energy proof without first converting the boundary to the raising-source dominance final callback. The fully threaded callback shape carries predicted-GS, real source-weight, sublattice-Casimir, and successor-support data for both the lowered-vector Marshall component and the site-sum callback. The complex-form `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`
is the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the
magnetization sector: the ground state of the un-dressed quantum
Heisenberg Hamiltonian restricted to the sector is unique (up to a
positive real scalar in its real part) and has the Marshall sign
structure `ΈΠ œÉ := ((sign A œÉ.1).re * v œÉ : β³²)` with `v > 0`.

`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` (PR #3337) is the **final-statement form**
of the `|A| βâ† |¬§A|` case. The hypothesis bundle matches
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`
(#869) exactly. The conclusion bundles, per admissible sector
`M βàà tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`, the Marshall-positive
sector eigenvector existence and within-sector uniqueness from
#869, together with a global energy-minimality clause. The
predicted total spin and predicted degeneracy enter as the
cardinality identity `|tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N| =
tasaki23PredictedDegeneracy A N = 2 S_tot + 1` with
`S_tot = ||A| βà£ |¬§A||¬ΖN/2 = tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N`. The
proof iterates #869 across `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`.

References: H. Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 (pp. 39βÄ™43), Theorem 2.3
(p. 42); E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains*
(3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ¬ß1.2 (pp. 27βÄ™28) for the underlying
PerronβÄ™Frobenius theorem.

### Spin-`S` saturated ferromagnetic state (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 generalised)

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 P1i for the
**saturated ferromagnet**: the all-aligned (constant-spin) basis
state `|œÉ_βäΛβü© = βä½_x |cβü©` with `œÉ x = c` for all `x : V`. The two
extremal weights `c = 0` (highest weight, "all up") and
`c = Fin.last N` (lowest weight, "all down") are the highest- and
lowest-weight vectors of the `J_tot = |V|¬ΖS = |V|¬ΖN/2` irreducible
SU(2) representation in the multi-site Hilbert space. Tracked in
Issue #412; assembled in PRs #875βÄ™#879. All theorems live in
`Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedState.lean`.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `allAlignedConfigS V N c` | constant configuration `œÉ x = c` (PR #875) |
| `allAlignedStateS V N c` | basis state at constant `c`, equal to `basisVecS (allAlignedConfigS V N c)` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS` | `magSumS = |V|¬Ζc.val` (PR #875) |
| `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` | `magEigenvalueS = |V|¬ΖN/2 βà£ |V|¬Ζc` (PR #875) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_allAlignedStateS` | `≈€^z_tot ¬Ζ |cβü© = (|V|¬ΖN/2 βà£ |V|¬Ζc) ¬Ζ |cβü©` for any `c` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | `c = 0` βüΙ `magSumS = 0` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_pos_of_ne_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | the all-up configuration is the **unique** `magSumS = 0` configuration (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS_last` | `c = Fin.last N` βüΙ `magSumS = |V|¬ΖN` (PR #876) |
| `magSumS_lt_card_mul_of_ne_allAlignedConfigS_last` | the all-down configuration is the unique configuration with `magSumS = |V|¬ΖN` (PR #876) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | the **all-up state is a Heisenberg eigenvector for ANY coupling** βÄî magnetization conservation `[H, ≈€^z_tot] = 0` + uniqueness of the M=0 configuration (PR #875) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_eigenvalue` | explicit Heisenberg eigenvalue formula on all-up: `ΈΘ_x J(x,x)¬ΖN(N+2)/4 + ΈΘ_{xβâ†y} J(x,y)¬ΖN¬≤/4` (PR #875) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` / `_eigenvalue` | symmetric c = N (all-down) Heisenberg eigenvector + same eigenvalue formula (PR #876) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpPlus_apply_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | `(onSiteS x ≈€^+) œÉ' œÉ_βäΛ = 0` (PR #877) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | `(onSiteS x ≈€^+).mulVec |œÉ_βäΛβü© = 0` (PR #877) |
| `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **`≈€^+_tot ¬Ζ |œÉ_βäΛβü© = 0`** (highest-weight annihilation, PR #877) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpMinus_apply_allAlignedConfigS_last` / `_mulVec_` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric lowest-weight annihilation `≈€^-_tot ¬Ζ |œÉ_βäΞβü© = 0` (PR #877) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | the all-up state is a `(≈€_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_apply_diag_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | explicit Casimir diagonal value `|V|¬ΖN(N+2)/4 + (|V|¬≤βà£|V|)¬ΖN¬≤/4` (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_eigenvalue` | **`(≈€_tot)¬≤ ¬Ζ |œÉ_βäΛβü© = (|V|¬ΖN/2)¬Ζ(|V|¬ΖN/2 + 1) ¬Ζ |œÉ_βäΛβü©`** βÄî operator-level form of "all-up is the highest-weight vector of the J_tot = |V|¬ΖS irreducible SU(2) representation" (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` / `_apply_diag_` / `_eigenvalue` | symmetric lowest-weight Casimir eigenvalue (same value) (PR #879) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSOp1` / `_Op2` / `_OpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | `Commute`-form conversions: H commutes with each axis-total operator (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow` / `_Plus_pow` | iterated power Commute by induction (PR #881) |

This packages the finite-sum positivity step behind Tasaki's equation (S.23); the remaining input is the concrete Marshall-gauge matrix-entry non-negativity and strict witness for the ground-state vector. For `A x βâ† A y`, the bipartite gauge sign and Marshall sign product cancel on every non-zero ladder matrix entry, leaving the bare non-negative entry; for `N βâΞ 1` an explicit two-configuration witness gives strict positivity. This feeds PR #4069's finite-sum positivity wrapper for strictly positive Marshall coefficients. If the signed `≈€_x^-≈€_y^+` real part matches the signed `≈€_x^+≈€_y^-` real part and the signed longitudinal `≈€_x^3≈€_y^3` real part is half of it, then signed ladder positivity implies signed dot-product positivity; combined with PR #4071 this gives the Marshall-coefficient cross-sublattice wrapper under those component equalities. A generic adjoint-expectation bridge shows that the expectation of `MβÄ†` is the complex conjugate of the expectation of `M`; since `(≈€_x^+≈€_y^-)βÄ† = ≈€_x^-≈€_y^+` for `x βâ† y`, the two ladder expectations are complex conjugates, and multiplication by the real cross-sublattice bipartite gauge sign preserves equality of real parts. The resulting wrapper combines PR #4071 and PR #4072 while leaving only the longitudinal `≈€_x^3≈€_y^3` equality as the remaining SU(2) input. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution p. 498, equations (S.22)-(S.23) (PR #4073, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dLadderAdjointEquality.lean`) |
| `twoSpinProductCorrelationS` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_spinSOp3_eq_twoSpinZZCorrelationS` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_eq_of_conj_phaseInvariant` / `conj_product_of_conj_factors` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_axis3_eq_axis2_of_axisSwap_phase` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_axis1_eq_axis2_of_zAxisRot_phase` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_axis1_add_axis2_eq_ladder` / `bipartite_signed_twoSpinZZCorrelationS_re_eq_half_plusMinus_of_axis_phases` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_cross_axis_phases` | **Problem 2.5.d longitudinal component equality**: discharges the remaining longitudinal SU(2) component equality left by PR #4073 under axis-swap and z-axis rotation phase invariance. The two-site phase-invariant bridge gives `C33 = C22` and `C11 = C22`; the `spinSOp3` bridge identifies `C33` with the existing `twoSpinZZCorrelationS`; the transverse ladder identity gives `C11 + C22 = 1/2(C^{+-} + C^{-+})`; PR #4073 gives equality of the signed ladder real parts, hence `Re(g C33) = 1/2 Re(g C^{+-})`. The resulting wrapper removes the final component-equality hypothesis from the signed dot-product positivity transfer. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23) (PR #4074, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dLongitudinalComponentEquality.lean`) |

This wrapper is for a full-space coefficient function; the sector-supported Perron-Frobenius ground-state shape is handled separately. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4075, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dGroundStatePhaseWrapper.lean`) |
| `twoSpinPlusMinusCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_coefficients` / `twoSpinPlusMinusCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross_components` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross_axis_phases` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross_eigenphase` | **Problem 2.5.d sector-supported wrapper**: adapts the PR #4069--#4075 positivity chain to the actual sector-supported Marshall-positive ground-state shape produced by Perron-Frobenius. A sector vector with strictly positive coefficients is zero-extended by `magSectorEmbedding`; off-sector terms in the ladder expectation vanish, on-sector terms are positive-coefficient multiples of the signed matrix entries, and the same axis-phase/eigenspace bridge supplies the signed dot-product positivity conclusion. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4076, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dSectorSupportedWrapper.lean`) |
| `twoSpinPlusMinus_ladder_signed_entry_re_pos_of_off_two_site_raise_lower` / `exists_twoSpinPlusMinus_ladder_signed_entry_re_pos_of_bipartite_ne_balanced_sector` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_balanced_sector_cross_eigenphase` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced-sector ladder witness**: supplies the strict sector-local `S_x^+ S_y^-` matrix-entry witness required by PR #4076 at the balanced sector `M0 = |A| * N`. The witness starts from the complement NΟ©el configuration, updates the two cross-sublattice sites touched by the ladder operator, and uses `magSumS_configUpdateTwo_eq` plus `magSumS_neelConfigOfS_complement` to keep both configurations in `M0`. The resulting wrapper removes the explicit strict-entry hypothesis from the sector-supported eigenspace-phase theorem at the balanced sector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4077, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedSectorWitness.lean`) |
| `phase_unit_of_unitary_mulVec_eq_smul_of_ne_zero` / `exists_phase_unit_of_finrank_eigenspace_le_one_of_unitary_commute_of_ne_zero` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius endpoint**: extracts the balanced sector PF vector from the Theorem 2.3 common-energy data, rewrites the existing real-sign embedding into the sector-supported Marshall form using `marshallSignS_eq_ofReal_re`, and derives the axis-swap / z-rotation unit-modulus phases without normalizing the vector. The endpoint returns the common Hermitian minimum, the strictly positive balanced coefficients, the non-zero embedded eigenvector, the full eigenspace `finrank βâΛ 1` input from the SU(2) endpoint, and the signed cross-sublattice two-spin correlation positivity for that concrete PF vector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4078, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFEndpoint.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_sign_cases_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius sign cases**: applies the final bipartite-gauge sign conversion to the concrete balanced PF endpoint from PR #4078. The wrapper preserves the Hermitian minimum, strictly positive balanced coefficients, non-zero embedded eigenvector, and full eigenspace `finrank βâΛ 1` package, while replacing the signed-positivity conclusion by the four Boolean same-sublattice positive / cross-sublattice negative sign cases for the original two-spin correlation. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4079, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFSignCases.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_re_neg_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius cross sign**: extracts the concrete non-vacuous cross-sublattice conclusion from PR #4079. Under `A x βâ† A y`, the Boolean sign cases collapse to the original two-spin correlation inequality `(twoSpinCorrelationS x y ΈΠ).re < 0` for the same balanced PF vector, while preserving the Hermitian minimum, positive coefficients, non-zero eigenvector, and `finrank βâΛ 1` package. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4080, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFCrossSign.lean`) |
| `spinSOpPlus_one_eq_spinHalfOpPlus` / `_Minus_` / `_Op1_` / `_Op2_` / `_Op3_` | **spin-`S` βÜî spin-`1/2` bridge at `N = 1`**: `spinSOp{Plus, Minus, 1, 2, 3} 1 = spinHalfOp{Plus, Minus, 1, 2, 3}` (each is the corresponding half-Pauli matrix) (PRs #922 + #923, file `Quantum/SpinS/SpinHalfSpecialization.lean`) |

### Single-mode fermion (P2 skeleton)

Phase 2 entry point: the canonical anticommutation algebra of a single
fermion mode acting on `β³²¬≤` with computational basis
`|0βü©` (vacuum) and `|1βü©` (occupied).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `fermionAnnihilation` | `c = !![0, 1; 0, 0] = |0βü©βü®1|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation` | `cβÄ† = !![0, 0; 1, 0] = |1βü©βü®0|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber` | `n = !![0, 0; 0, 1] = |1βü©βü®1|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber_eq_creation_mul_annihilation` | `n = cβÄ† ¬Ζ c` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_sq` | `c¬≤ = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation_sq` | `(cβÄ†)¬≤ = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
